Frontier regions have maybe 1-2 million. Orre has something like 10 million. All in all, I’d say there’s only around a billion humans worldwide.

Question: How serious is power creep? Like, obviously there were big jumps with TMs and Pokeballs. But other than that, what's it like?
Answer: Major power creep over the last centuries due to more peace and time to train and progression in science, but the biggest appeared with TMs and Pokeballs (pokeballs being the largest due to eliminating many of the logistical problems associated with raising multiple Pokémon). TMs appeared less than twenty years ago and instantly changed the battle circuit. Power creep is pretty much stagnant at this point, with only innovations like multiple use TMs becoming more accessible. It’s mostly leveled off at this point.

Question: On Super Speed’s origins.
Answer: If I remember right, Super Speed is Ash’s design. Inspired by Lance’s emphasis on speed and maneuverability, but not created by Lance.

Question: Other people have probably made similar things, correct?
Answer: Yes, there are plenty of speed boosting techniques, just not in that same regard. It’s not terribly practical for things other than Pidgeot of[or] other incredibly fast fliers - otherwise it’s usually best to enhance other aspects. Agility does the job perfectly well most of the time.

Question: Are the other game protagonists only a year younger than Ash?
Answer: Brendan is about a year and a half younger, Lucas is a little over two years younger, going on with the others. I’m staggering them each a year for the most part.

Question: How old would Ash be by the time Hilda started traveling?
Answer: He’d be...15ish? I do have Unova starting at early to mid teens due to the unrest after the Last War. It wasn’t a very safe environment then and the age sort of stuck.

Question: Is Hilda closer to Ash’s age since her starting point is staggered more behind him?
Answer: Yep, she’ll be either a year younger or the same age as him.

Question: Was what [redacted] said about the damage to ash if he tried mustering charge what you had in mind, if he tries to use Lightning for anything beyond what amounts to forcing his limbs to carry him forwards like in Greenfield?
Answer: I won’t say too much on it [redacted] but I will say it is theoretically possible for Ash to channel that much electricity. Won’t go into details at this point.

Question: How has Orre's ecosystem recovered after the events of xd?
Answer: Orre’s still a blasted wasteland, but the oases have continued to expand. The main reason pokemon have fled the region in the past is due to hostile human presence, particularly those that would capture and exploit them for gang wars and the like. Some oases have been redevastated as Orre descends more and more into anarchy, but Duking has been able to protect several as well and those have thrived. It’s done a lot in terms of restoring the human/Pokémon relationship in the region.

Question: Is it [Orre] home to more than 9 unique species now?
Answer: Yeah, I’m treating the 9 species as just a gameplay mechanic. Although it is evident of certain Pokémon common to the area.

Question: Does Infernus have limited psychic powers? Like, in general could he take them further then they are now and is it worth it?
Answer: Infernus does have limited psychic powers. They can be taken further, although considering his other talents they’d best be used to enhance his flames at this point. I have some plans for it in the future!

Question: How would ghost/dark type moves affect the psychic abilities of a normal type? Would they still disrupt them?
Answer: Yep. Ghost and dark type moves are antithetical to psychic abilities.

Question: Do gym leaders have to specialize in one pokemon type? If they do, can they change the gym type if they become a leader?
Answer: Gyms tend to have a historical basis for a certain type specialty, but there isn’t a required type. Most trainers on that level tend to be specialists, however. Oftentimes a Gym Leader will adapt their team to fit a city’s historic focus, or at least capture an additional Pokémon or two of that type.

Question: Do you have a preferred last name for Agatha?
Answer: Hashimoto for the purposes of your story.

Question: What is Kanto's Square Footage?
Answer: Most regions are the size of an average sized US western state - I don’t give exact numbers, but 50-70000 square miles. That tends to be several hundred miles across and up and down, which Plume can very, very easily cover in a day even at a reduced speed that Ash can take. On long trips without much need for maneuvering she can fly faster as well.

Question: What concept could Genesect represent?
Answer: Unova is fun. For Genesect, each will basically be on the level of Steven’s Metagross minus psychic abilities. They’re essential what Giovanni should have done: powerful, reliably produced, controllable super soldiers. Not Legends, but their modifications make them extremely formidable. They’re force multipliers rather than nukes such as Mewtwo. They’re reliable.

Question: You said Genesect were reliably made but how reliably were they made?
Answer: They’re extremely limited in number due to limitations on the number of specimens they can get to perform the process on, the process of augmenting Genesect killing nearly 3/4 of the attempts until it was more fine tuned, and the biggest factor comes down to cost. It’s ridiculously expensive to clone, it’s ridiculously expensive to augment them, and it’s ridiculously expensive to maintain and keep Genesect alive and healthy. They’re extremely resource intensive.

Question: Back with Mewtwo, it was mentioned that he had some trouble grasping dark types were harder to grasp at with his powers. Now considering that Suicune was able to sense Alakazam manipulating the universe, presumably with Suicune’s own powers, would Suicune have the ability to psychically manipulate Karen’s Absol if it was mega evolved? Additionally, since the manipulation of water is considered to be specialised psychic abilities, could Suicune manipulate water that Darkrai had enveloped?
Answer: One thing to remember is that it’s entirely different for Legends. Suicune’s abilities are similar to psychic powers, but it senses Alakazam through the wind and rain. In practice it’s like a massive psychic awareness, but it’s different in that it’s innately tied to the universe. Psychic abilities stand out as flashes, so it was able to sense Alakazam’s intentions and attempt.

Question: Could you clarify the example that [redacted] talked about the corrupted water and would Suicune be able to manipulate it? [see question 217]
Answer: If it was actively in contact with Darkrai, no. But if Darkrai left, it would be able to purge the corruption with a great deal of focus.

Question: Is the corrupted water just a byproduct from Darkrai or does he actively corrupt it? [see question 218]
Answer: Just in the area, and it’s a byproduct of Darkrai’s existence.

Question: Would that mean Darkrai would be more powerful then Suicune? [see question 219]
Answer: It’s not really about power in this case. They just have different natures and different influences on the world.

Question: Isn't Lance the strongest, and Cynthia the most skilled?
Answer: Yeah, Lance has an edge over Cynthia but they’re basically even. Cynthia beats him out in versatility and she can beat him fairly evenly, but he’s a harder fight for her than Steven is - Lance is fairly unpredictable and wild, which makes it harder for Cynthia’s team to pick him apart like she can Steven.

Question: What's the differences between the various poisons used by pokemon? Like is Nidoking's venom different from a Seviper or Tentacruel and is Seviper and Arbok venom the same or Garbador and Muk sludge etc.? Can Weezing produce different types of poison gas?
Answer: Sorry for the wait, I missed this one. There are definitely different kinds of poisons, although I imagine them to be fairly similar in structure with just minor variations for the most part. Most Pokémon venom or poison is ridiculously effective.

Question: What did Michael do differently from Wes? Does Micheal keep his full team on him at all times or in orre does he fight fully lethal "go for the trainers"?
Answer: And basically, the explanation above is correct. Wes and Rui cut off an arm. They dealt massive damage that took years to recover from, but the organization survived. Michael cut off the head with Greevil.

Question: Do gym leaders use their true teams on challengers when they are no longer rookies or have participated in a League Conference? Or both? And speaking of the Conference there were mentions of repeat competitors. So are Badges earned in one season valid for only tha Conference of the same year or is it earned once,lifetime qualifications situation?
Answer: It’s essentially up to the Gym Leader’s discretion. In general they match their teams to their opponent. You’ll have a few - such as Sabrina, Blaine, and Giovanni - who always fight at a higher level. They’re the gatekeepers to the Conference. But Gym Leaders ten[d]s to fight around their opponents level - people with no badges get newer or inexperienced opponents, while they gradually scale up.

Question: On the Indigo Conference’s bracket.
Answer: Yep, for trainers that didn’t get all 8 main badges they had to go through admission rounds to whittle the numbers down for the actual Conference.

Question: When do the admission rounds happen? [see question 225]
Answer: The weeks before.

Question: What's the information regarding pseudo-legendaries and how "special" they are compared to normal Pokemon?
Answer: Pseudo-legendaries are naturally powerful Pokémon. As wild Pokémon they’ll tend to be unmatched and claim a status as apex predators. At the lower and middle tiers of battle they’re pretty much unstoppable due to their natural advantages. Just look at Pierce’s Metagross.

Question: On whether Michael fought Mewtwo.
Answer: Michael fought Giovanni before Mewtwo was brought in. It took a while before Mewtwo could regulate his power enough to ‘safely’ fight normal trainers.

Question: Aside from Lucario/Riolu other Pokemon can learn Aura Sphere. Is the reason the same as Lucario, that they have an innate ability to control aura as well?
Answer: In Traveler, Aura Sphere is more a raw expression of Aura. It’s a commonality to many Pokémon and humans who know of Aura - either through extensive self-reflection or because their species have communities that pass down knowledge of Aura - because it’s simple yet effective. It’s a physical manifestation of a desire or need to attack.

Question: Does Lance know about the aura?
Answer: Lance does know about Aura.

Question: Does the Bond Phenomenon come into play at all, and if it does, does it have anything to do with Aura?
Answer: We likely won’t see something exactly like Bond Phenomenon in exact terms of how Ash/Greninja interacted, but it does have to do with Aura.

Question: On Aura.
Answer: In Traveler, Aura is essentially self expression. It’s using a part of yourself and expressing it physically into the world. Bruno has dedicated his life to training and combat, and as a result he can fortify his body to superhuman levels. Cynthia seeks knowledge and spent her life working with her team, so she has Aura abilities enhancing those aspects of her character.

Question: I assume Ash will eventually at least study it[Aura]?
Answer: Sort of. He’s studying it right now in a way.

Question: So the question is for those[Legendary Pokemon] that embody a concept, were they created already representing their respective concept or were they given a role and modelled themselves after it?
Answer: I won’t give a hard rule, but you can think of Legends as an entity built around a Concept. They’re the expression of a Concept in the physical world.

Question: On Ash’s team.
Answer: So this won’t help in the meantime, but as of this moment Ash is going to end his journey with 14 Pokémon. Just for something to refer to later on.

Question: How likely is a pokemon like skarmory to accidentally harm its trainer?
Answer: Fairly unlikely. It can happen (especially if Skarmory is freshly caught and unused to taking care) but in general Skarmory are aware of how dangerous they can be to their trainer and take steps to prevent any injuries.

Question: Flying on one is more hazardous though? [see question 236]
Answer: Oh yeah. It requires specialized equipment to keep a trainer from being sliced to ribbons.

Question: I assume that starter pokemon are endangered and have breeding programs/ranches?
Answer: Sort of. They exist in the wild nowadays, but mostly on preserves due to serious loss of population in the past. It depends on the region, however.

Question: So this just popped into my head. Can a Jigglypuff or etc's Sing be controlled enough to selectively put to sleep opponents without affecting others in hearing range? And can it be recorded to put others to sleep?
Answer: Good question! That definitely depends. I see Sing as having a psychic component that makes it more effective, which can be controlled by a skilled Jigglypuff. It’s fairly difficult, however, and requires a lot of focus to not just ‘broadcast’.

Question: What's the difference between mental arts and physical manipulation?
Answer: Mental arts involve manipulation of emotions, thoughts, and perceptions via psychic influence. Physical manipulation is the projection and creation of force, barriers, and finer methods of manipulating matter, energy, and force.

Question: Can psychic pokemon be good at both mental art and physical manipulation?
Answer: Yep. Just look at Dazed, whose species specializes in mental arts yet she’s incredibly powerful with physical manipulation through extensive training. Alakazam and several other species (Espeon and Gardevoir for example) are also good at both.

Question: Why do we need Bob to teleport Ash, when he can take help from Dazed?
Answer: Dazed isn’t really capable of long range teleportation. She could do a few miles at best (without being juiced up on Mewtwo’s power, which she wants to avoid for obvious reasons haha). Even most Alakazam have to do jumps for cross-region travel. Members of the Teleportation Squad are incredibly specialized in teleporting and are capable of jumping across entire regions by themselves in a single teleport.

Question: Can it[Sabrina’s Alakazam] teleport long range?
Answer: Yep. Sabrina’s Alakazam is basically a Master of All when it comes to psychic abilities. It’s got similar physical abilities to a powerful Metagross (not Steven’s, of course), excellent mental abilities, and great space-time manipulation.

Question: Has anyone ever tried to build a mecha for a Rotom to possess? Is that legal for a regulated match? In fact, can Rotom possessed appliances be stored in pokeballs and used for matches?
Answer: I have Rotom as being fairly elusive and mysterious - not unknown, but quite rare at this point in time (especially in the National League). There hasn’t been a ton of research into them, although I can definitely see powerful bodies being built for them by the League once they reach that point. Rotom in appliances can be used for matches - I figure if something as awful as Cynthia’s Spiritomb can be used a Rotom can be as well haha.

Question: Are there Elite Four or Pokemon League insignias?
RelatedTags: ['Elite Four', 'Hoenn', 'Indigo League', 'League', 'Lily of the Valley', 'National League', 'Rhydon', 'Salamence', 'Sinnoh']
Answer: So the National League’s insignia is just a stylized pokeball, Indigo’s is a circle with a bisected Rhydon’s head, Sinnoh’s is a stylized Lily of the valley, and Hoenn’s is a Salamence.

Question: On the insignia used by the Indigo League.
Answer: Indigo DOES heavily use the Flame of Moltres as a symbol, however. It’s not the official insignia, but it has a lot of history and power in Kanto and Johto.

Question: On the Dark World’s inaccessibility.
Answer: No Champions or ranking League members have tried to enter the Dark World in centuries - people have tried to explore for thousands of years, but at this point it’s a known death sentence. Occasional explorers still enter, but they’re never seen again.

Question: Would Unova’s [insignia] be a Braviary? [see question 245]
Answer: I’ll have to think about Kalos’ but yeah, Braviary is the current symbol. Under the former regime it was a Hydreigon.

Question: Can Humans travel to outer space?
Answer: Humans can go to outer space and have sent satellites up, but they have a fairly low success rate - quite a few tend to be mysteriously destroyed.

Question: What happens to the feather when the bearer dies?
Answer: In general, the Feather will dissipate unless the Bird desires it to be passed on to a new bearer. Tani’s Feather faded away when she died, Lance’s would most likely do the same (unless he died as Champion, in which case Moltres would likely desire for the Feather to be picked up by someone new and the covenant renewed). Haukea’s would remain in the world, but anyone who picked it up would regret it.

Question: On why Blast Burn is frequently taught using a TM.
Answer: I will say that a proper Blast Burn has an element of technique and control to it that is VERY difficult for a fire-type to discover and master on their own. There are several ‘forms’ of Blast Burn, each acting as a stage that preps the fire-type for the next until eventually you arrive at the final product. There’s a reason it’s a Master-level technique.

Question: Presumably Jonathan was at the very first stage, then? [see question 251]
Answer: Yep. Jonathan walked away with a very powerful technique, but he doesn’t have the full product yet.

Question: So could a Torkoal learn Blast Burn?
Answer: In Traveler, pretty much any fire-type can learn Blast Burn. Raw power helps, but it’s only one piece of the puzzle. I see it being restricted to starters as purely a game mechanic.

Question: Can any nota[b]le non fire types learn it[Blast Burn]? Does groudon have access to bb[Blast Burn] potentially?
Answer: Technically a non fire-type could learn it, but it would be extraordinarily difficult. They also lack the innate resistance to heat and flame that a fire-type does, so it would be damaging to them in most cases. As for Groudon, it and the rest of the Weather Trio are in the boat where they don’t need techniques.

Question: Would any fire types be able to use Blast Burn naturally?
Answer: Nah, Blast Burn absolutely requires a great deal of experience and skill to wield, although some Pokémon might have an easier time learning it.

Question: If Magnus doesn't know how to do it [Blast Burn], I'll eat Ash's hat.
Answer: Magnus does know it!

Question: How prevalent is cannibalism in the pokemon world? Do mothers eat there eggs in desperate times? Do ariados females eat their mates?
Answer: It happens, but generally only out of necessity unless a Pokémon is very, very disturbed...at which point other wild Pokémon will probably gang up to kill it anyways.

Question: So Pierce’s Umbreon, did it eat other Umbreons? Is it that disturbed?
Answer: It probably would if it needed to in order to survive, but in general it preferred killing to those acts.

Question: What about Precipice Blades or Origin Pulse or Dragon Ascent? Are those exceptions? [see question 254]
Answer: Sort of. In Traveler, their[Weather Trio’s] techniques are more along the lines of them just focusing their attention for a moment rather than just letting their power emanate out.

Question: How would you describe rare candies in Traveler verse? I know Gary made a joke about Ash's team being injected with them so are they like steroids?
Answer: Pretty much. I imagine them being used for physical enhancement, although best used in small doses to prevent physiological dependencies and complications. Additionally, using them can lead to a Pokémon losing coordination due to rapid growth of muscle (and sometimes size in general). Few high tier trainers use it for these reasons, as well as it being very much a crutch. Without natural growth, the Pokémon possess less skill and tend to have weaker abilities.

Question: So they're [Rare Candies are] essentially steroids? [see question 260]
Answer: Basically lol...but with super Pokémon science!

Question: Does Bruiser have a belt around his waist and how is the belt around his neck tied, like the knot behind is back or do they hang down on his front?
Answer: So Bruiser wears the standard Machoke power save belt around his waist. The black belt is tied around his neck loosely with the knot in front.

Question: Are there any limitations to the move Mimic? Will a Mightyena exhaust itself when copying a Draco Meteor or something?
Answer: Oh yeah. Mimic can be useful but is seriously limited, especially with more powerful Pokémon. High level techniques will lack a lot of the power if used by a Pokémon of a different type who hasn’t trained in those sorts of technique, for example. Or if they are able to ‘brute force’ it to keep the same power as the original technique, it’ll be extremely inefficient.

Question: On what Mew’s role as the “Guardian of Life” means.
Answer: You’re pretty much correct. Basically the ‘Guardian of Life’ is looking at all life rather than individual organisms or species. Mew ensures life as a whole continues, although it’s also essentially had to reboot organic life several times due to Legendary conflicts.

Question: Any thoughts on mining in Traveler-verse?
Answer: I can’t answer everything right now but I’ll address a few of the recent questions. Mining exists, and is usually reliant with negotiations made by the League with wild Pokémon. In the past it was more aggressive, but that was a disaster for all involved. Fossil fuels have been heavily used in the past, particularly at the advent of the industrial era, but led to serious issues with wild Pokémon. Pollution heavily irritates local populations, and they’re more than happy to take a detour to sabotage whatever strange technology the humans are using.

Question: I'd imagine steel use would have to be much lower, right? [see question 265]
Answer: Yep. Mining has been an essential part of civilization since the beginning, even with the presence of Pokémon providing many, many other advantages that let them work around common challenges early civilizations faced in our world. The Stone family, for example, carved out its current high place in Rustboro by using their Beldum colony to safely mine areas. They essentially held a monopoly on it for many years.

Question: On Traveler verse’s tree biology.
Answer: Trees and plants tend to grow slightly faster in Traveler’s world as well. Just an adaptation for living in a world with superpowered monsters haha. But yeah, tree farms [see question 266] are mostly how it’s done. Actual logging and deforestation is a nightmare to deal with - taking territory from wild Pokémon without regard just sows the seed for conflict.

Question: Do berries grow really quickly? [see question 267]
Answer: Yep. It’s not like they’ll grow overnight, but they have evolved to grow rapidly in order to spread as widely as possible - which is definitely helped by the huge nutritional requirements of most pokemon.

Question: How does a Shedinja's Wonder Guard work in Traveler? Does it just have a force field around it?
Answer: So, essentially Shedinja is the discarded carapace of Nincada that is possessed by a Distortional entity. The carapace is extremely fragile, and as a result a massive amount of the Shedinja’s power is devoted solely to defense, essentially a shroud that nullifies almost any attack. It also invests a large portion of its energy into fortifying its shell. There are only a handful of weaknesses:

Question: Does a well-trained one[Shedinja] focus on minimizing the moments of vulnerability? [see question 269]
Answer: Yep. Strength in Shedinja honestly comes down mostly to mastering themselves and reducing the chinks in their armor.

Question: Is Mewtwo hiding at Mt. Quena again as per canon?
Answer: Nope, he has no need since he’s remained at New Island.

Question: There was no elite with Metagross [during the Last War]? And how rare are Metagross actually? Is Stone colony only one?
Answer: Well, Steven’s Metagross to be specific. There were probably decent numbers of Metagross deployed by Hoenn. They’re very valuable in that situation due to their innate durability and psychic prowess.

Question: How thorough is [Steven’s] Metagross's physics knowledge?
Answer: Metagross have very good physics knowledge. They see much more through their psychic awareness than their eyes, which gives a massive rush of data/information from the environment (which is also how Steven’s can snipe fliers so well). With their brains, they can interpret that data very effectively  and it leads to an intuitive comprehension of physics.

Question: Are they[Porygon] used in battle?
Answer: Yep, although they’re uncommon. Honestly, using them in battle is probably the least effective way to utilize them.

Question: Are Porygon utilised for general computation, Simulation based computation, or simply security?
Answer: Pretty much everything. Different Porygon are trained in different ways but they’re very flexible.

Question: Are they[Porygon] used for digital security by League?
Answer: They are used by the League.

Question: When an Exeggutor dies naturally, what happens to its eggs?
Answer: They’re powerful enough that weaker predators would ignore them out of fear, but plenty of Pokémon would like to snatch up a dropped head.

Question: Do you think that ditto cells within Traveler will lead towards age, non-lethal injury and ailment immortality?
Answer: Yep. Ages will definitely vastly increase. It’ll be something that contributes to serious overpopulation in the future, pushes for expansion, and inevitable conflict with wild Pokémon unless the League regulates it.

Question: Does Mewtwo even require sleep?
Answer: If you took away his vast psychic abilities, he would need only minimal sleep due to the genetic engineering. But with psychic powers he doesn’t need it - he’s basically capable of passively ‘refreshing’ his brain to keep him well-rested. He’s escaped most biological needs.

Question: How skilled a psychic is Mewtwo honestly?
Answer: Basically Mewtwo is about as skilled as he needs to be. With the ridiculous level of psychic power, he’s capable of manipulating the world on micro and macro scales. But in a fight he’s going to default to simple blasts, beams, and grips for the most part. Time is a valuable resource, and even a tiny delay in creating a more complex effect might not be worth it. Not against something as powerful as a Legend anyway.

Question: Given what we know of pseudo-legendaries and how powerful they are, what is a Slaking like in Traveler given it's versatile moveset, insane bulk attack, good defense and speed and special attack and how does Truant measure up in Traveler? Are they just lazy super weapons? And has there been a normal master or champion with a Slaking cornerstone?
Answer: Slaking are known as absolute beasts. Very difficult to train since they tend to be fairly unmotivated, but their natural power makes them forces to be reckoned with. Their biggest issue is stamina. They overheat easily, and are best used for explosive bursts. They tend to focus on elemental attacks for longer battles. There have definitely been powerful leaders in Hoenn with Slaking, but the League is too recent to have had Champions with it.

Question: Is the duel not accepted if one of the trainers doesn't have the money to pay it off then?
Answer: If a trainer tries to battle without having above the minimum balance, it’ll either be a battle just for fun or be declined.

Question: On Galar and Kalos’ shared history.
Answer: I have Galar as the war Kalos went to war with in ancient times. Figured it’d be fitting with the French/British rivalry.

Question: What's the Galar League like at the moment? Is Leon the champion?
Answer: Leon isn’t Champion yet, but he will be in the next few years.

Question: On whether Pokemon can use Jirachi.
Answer: I won’t get into the rest, but Pokemon are able to use Jirachi.

Question: How does Pokémon's water taste? Is it drinkable for humans?
Answer: For the most part I'd say it is drinkable.

Question: Can aquatic pokemon survive in both fresh and saltwater, are there any exceptions?
Answer: Ooh that’s an interesting one. I’d say it depends. We see a ton of Pokémon that are able to exist in both salt and freshwater environments, so we know a bunch are able to survive in both environments. With how tough Pokémon are, I think we could say that many species are able to adapt somewhat to their environment. There are probably many who prefer either freshwater or saltwater, but can at least survive for a time outside of their preferred environment.

Question: How much does rampage amp up his[Bruiser’s] movement speed anyways?
Answer: So, Rampage makes Bruiser a fair bit faster. He’s still not very maneuverable, but he covers ground quickly and you wouldn’t want him coming at you in a straight line. His jumps can carry him pretty far.

Question: On Air Lens’ vulnerability.
Answer: Air Lens can be learned by certain other fire-types (mostly those capable of naturally projecting extreme body heat) but I will say a true Electric Master wouldn’t be overly stymied by it. It’s very difficult to overpower, but there are ways around it.

Question: Since magnezones are steel/electric types can they potentially pass currents through themselves to create polarity and positive to negative potential to make a Faraday Cage and become immune to electricity?
Answer: I don’t know the science well enough to say anything for real, but I figure sure. It would probably be a Master-level technique, though, and they’d have easier options at that point.

Question: On Pokemon types.
Answer: Looking at types, they aren’t really scientific classification IMO so much as convenient categorizations for trainers. Poison is just a general term.

Question: So would more biological steel types be affected by poison in Traveler or is there a catch all of the steel type that grants an immunity?
Answer: Steel types like Magneton aren’t entirely immune - they just have an extreme resistance due to their armor.

Question: How to OKHO moves work in Traveler? Are they just very devastating moves in general? And is explosion/self destruct a move that is fatal to the user or do any mons that learn it have a biological quirk that lets them survive?
Answer: OHKO are just extremely devastating. Self-destruct has both fatal (seen in the Rocket Pokémon in Chapter...39?) and non lethal versions.

Question: Are all pokemon species protected by League laws or are there some species more pest-like that tend to be exterminated (like in cities)?
Answer: League doesn’t have extermination orders unless things are extremely desperate. It’s a good way to end up like the Southern Continent.

Question: Are egg groups the same as in the games btw in Traveler? Or is it limited to just those of the same species or similar species?
Answer: Let’s just say most things make eggs for some reason and leave it at that. Egg groups will remain the same! Ghost reproduction is an exception - it can get weird.

Question: Does crystal onix exist in Traveler?
Answer: Yep.

Question: Do the Indigo or Orange League know about it[Crystal Onix]? [see question 296]
Answer: Yep, I imagine the Orange League knows about it and protects it. I can see it basically being a member of a rare sub-species. Orange!Onix?

Question: I wonder if it's[Crystal Onyx] kind of like the shiny gene?
Answer: Honestly, with how different it [Crystal Onyx] is from regular Onix I’d say it’s more likely to be an entirely different sub-species than a simple one-off mutation.

Question: When it comes to a missing limb (say Ash lost his left arm), do they replace the limb with a biological prosthetic grown via ditto cells? Or do they use ditto cells to somehow grow a new arm in its place?
Answer: I just skimmed the rest of the discussion and I’ll try to answer the main questions! Let me know if I miss anything.

Question: On Ditto.
Answer: Let’s just say Ditto has some help in the whole ‘DNA amplification and arrangement’ by virtue of being a failed clone of Mew. It kept some semblance of the instincts. Skilled Ditto are able to mold their form without DNA samples to resemble inanimate objects, but it is very, very difficult to hold it for long and is mostly used as a disguise.

Question: If Dittos can transform their body structures completely, wouldn't that imply that if such a change was forced on Ditto cells by outside stimuli, like using Ditto cells to regrow an arm in a lab for example, that the change would either be resisted or temporary?
Answer: Essentially, Ditto cells separated from the main intelligence simply act as pluripotent stem cells that adapt to whatever they are in contact with. Honestly, one way I’m using to avoid the whole ‘blatantly breaking conservation of mass’ and change it into ‘slightly breaking conservation of mass’ is to have Ditto much, much heavier than in canon. They’ll weigh several hundred pounds, which is enough to properly copy and adapt to most pokemon. They’ll have serious issues with larger Pokémon. I’m passing on the question about being able to breed with humans, but they can transform into them. It’s very uncomfortable and not good for their mental state, however. It’s against their nature and they don’t hold it for more than a few seconds in most cases.

Question: What does ash wear? How does it look? Simple t-shirt and pants?
Answer: Yep. He wears shorts pretty often in Hoenn (unless he’s going through rough territory) and normally just a plain shirt. And the hat of course.

Question: On the long line in the south western part of the Traveler world map.
Answer: I will say that long straight line is intentional.

Question: Do we know if the map is the whole world minus the poles? or is it just part of it?
Answer: The map doesn’t show a lot of the Southern Continent or the northernmost areas.

Question: On Heatran.
Answer: Heatran is a proper Legend in Traveler!

Question: Was rereading one of the early chapters and I was wondering, what sort of things are trainers taught before setting out on their journey?
Answer: A lot of the early chapters are subject to early installment weirdness/lack of forethought, but all trainers are required to pass a certification course that covers basic survival skills, training protocol, and laws. They’re also required to get vaccinations and other medical care to make them a little less vulnerable.

Question: On Shadow Pokemon.
Answer: I will say that Shadow Pokemon aren’t based on dark/ghost energies (although those can be used in the process).

Question: Are there any Victory Roads in Traveler?
Answer: Victory Roads do exist in some form, generally as wild areas maintained for training purposes near the Leagues.

Question: How potent are techniques like Attract in Traveler?
Answer: So basically Attract varies a lot from Pokémon to Pokémon. It’s most effective on Pokémon of similar biology and egg groups, although most organic Pokémon can use it to some effect against other organic Pokémon. Certain Pokémon can outright shrug it off. What does help is that there’s a powerful psychic component to it in addition to the mass release of pheromones, which offers a lot more flexibility to the technique.

Question: Who would do a better job of using Attract, providing there aren't any other independent variables? A Psychic type who's reliant on the mental proportions of the move, or a Fighting or Grass type primarily utilising the pheromone aspect?
Answer: I’d say it does depend on the Pokémon. The psychic is probably going to be able to cast the widest net and ensnare many different Pokémon, including nonorganic if it’s able to manipulate their minds properly. I think Pokémon primarily using the pheromone aspect would have it be more powerful on specific Pokémon (particularly dark-types).

Question: So psychic has versatility (minus Dark) and natural producers have efficiency it looks like. [see question 310]
Answer: Yep, good way to look at it. And there are particularly potent psychics who have developed incredible skill with mental techniques like that and can match natural producers - with psychic powers it really ends up being a matter of specialization.

Question: Given that ghosts with more formless or incorporeal proportions carry psychic abilities (Gengar, Mismagius) is there an argument for why they can't use Attract?
Answer: Sort of - certain ghosts in Traveler can absolutely mimic Attract’s effects. Quite a few have very potent abilities to influence minds. In Traveler, they’re incapable of using actual psychic abilities but are able to use Distortion-based power for similar effects.

Question: Instead of a control-and-order based system psychics use, ghosts might be using a persuasion basis?
Answer: That’s a very interesting, well thought out perspective! I’d love to see that implemented in a fic! I haven’t seen  anyone introduce rules like that before. Traveler’s rules are a bit different when it comes to ghosts - basically, ghosts are able to manipulate Distortional energy in the same way that psychics can manipulate psychic energy. Distortional energy can be precisely manipulated to achieve effects similar to psychic energy, but it has the nasty side-effect of breaking down whatever it’s in contact with.

Question: How are we defining Mythical at this point? There’s really not many similarities between most of the species. Like, there’s a ton of Shaymin. What makes them special compared to, like, Chikorita?
Answer: Yeah, a lot of the recent Mythical Pokémon are more ‘rare, powerful Pokémon’ than Legends in my eyes, and that’s going to color how they are adapted to Traveler. There are exceptions of course!

Question: Are there any actual cults in the pokemon world? Any major religions?
Answer: So, there isn’t really a large spanning religion in the Pokémon World. It highly depends on the region and tends to be localized, based upon the Legendaries they were exposed to (although pseudo-Legends such as Mamoru, the Mt. Moon Clefable, or Chinatsu might also be worshipped as local gods).

Question: Can we get spoilers on how Dazed is going to be developing power-wise cuz I did not appreciate her getting trucked by Pryce's Mamoswine?
Answer: I won’t go into detail, but on top of her mental manipulation increasing she’ll also learn to manipulate the world more adeptly. It’ll give her a little bit of elemental versatility.

Question: What’s the approximate worth of the currency within Traveler compared to a currency we have? Additionally, do you price match the game for pokeballs?
Answer: Yep, I’m using a poke to dollar rough equivalency. The games definitely use it based off of Yen from my research, but I felt that making it equivalent to dollars gave a better impact of the profit in a potentially dangerous career such as training.

Question: Hey Elf, what are your thoughts on subspecies? Like, would a Nidoran native to the Safari Zone be any different than one native to Mount Moon in any way?
Answer: Hmm. I mostly consider subspecies that would develop in isolated areas. It takes a lot of time, and I feel like most of the subspecies would just end up being regional variants in Traveler. There might be subtle differences worth noting, but nothing too dramatic.

Question: On trade routes.
Answer: Really like that write up! One thing to note is that traditional trade routes and physical travel are essentially dead at this point due to common teleportation and extremely convenient matter-energy conversion in the form of pokeball technology, which allows mass transport of goods for almost no cost. Most travel between cities is done by teleportation for safety and convenience. Planes and ferries and such do exist for inter regional travel, although there are some ‘taxi’ services affiliated with the League that maintain jump stations across the oceans.

Question: Did Mewtwo’s power cause extra interference in travel for Psychic types?
Answer: Mewtwo’s power is potent enough to seriously disturb teleportation if you aren’t a skilled psychic. Even skilled psychic’s essentially get drunk off it if they aren’t accustomed to it, such as Bob. By a day or two later it still permeates the world in that area enough to be easily accessible to normal psychics to draw on.

Question: On the consequences of a broken transportation infrastructure.
Answer: Honestly, I think that’s where Pokémon come in. Even with infrastructure heavily damaged, I can imagine specialized rock and ground-types able to construct rudimentary shelters and infrastructure. Nothing fancy or particularly long-lasting, but enough to get by until construction crews are able to offer a more permanent solution.

Question: On how the reappearance of Team Rocket affected public safety.
Answer: It's definitely a complicated system - most of the time the world is reasonably safe even for young trainers due to League efforts to keep close contact with wild Pokémon, psychics and Ranger scouts surveying the wilderness to keep an eye on things, and most dangerous Pokémon having been killed or forced out over the centuries. Until human problems like the Rockets make an appearance things are pretty idyllic.

Question: I vaguely remember there being an old anime episode(Grampa Canyon, I believe? Main thing I remember is Charmeleon evolving haha) where they found a bunch of fossil Pokemon still living inside the caverns. Are there any cases of that kind of thing in Traveler?
Answer: Good question! Honestly, I’m not a fan of that idea just because of how unrealistic it is for the fossil Pokémon to have survived in a tiny community like that for so long...but I also hate just outright denying stuff like that was in the anime. I’d probably try to spin it off as a refuge for released fossil Pokémon, honestly. Maybe a research project designed to see how they’d interact in the wild? I’d have to put some more thought into it.

Question: Will the Aether foundation make an appearance anytime in the near future?
Answer: I might make reference to Aether, but honestly I’m not sure if Traveler will reach that far. I’d like for Ash to at least visit every main region, so they’ll likely make some sort of appearance.

Question: If you rank how old each league is, does it follow the order of the games?
Answer: Not really! I did have Indigo as the first as a reference to the original gens, but Hoenn is actually one of the newest Leagues after existing as separate city-states since the fall of the Volumo Empire a little over a thousand years ago. I based them a little on Italy. Sinnoh has ancient civilization, but also a fairly old League. Unova’s League is relatively new, while Kalos incorporated the League model after the Last War into their traditional aristocracy.

Question: What regions would you say have the rockiest relationship with Kanto besides Orre?
Answer: That’s a hard one. Most major powers are pretty warm with the new generation of Champions that came in the last decade or so - most are highly idealistic, having been born during or shortly after the Last War and raised on the stories of its horrors. There’s still some bitterness between older generations of Unovans and Kanto, but that’s mostly overshadowed due to Alder’s new League essentially purging the former government from its ranks.

Question: There is also quite a bit of tension between Hoenn and Unova still, right?
Answer: Similar situation to Kanto [see question 326]. Hoenn suffered under Unova, but during the Unovan Restoration they built closer ties. Drake was hard on them, but fair to the new government and wise enough to see the necessity of building war torn Unova up instead of tearing it further down as some of his command desired.

Question: Assuming they follow the same lives, what happened to Ash’s pokemon in the original anime that were abused/abandoned by their original trainers? Like Charmander, for example.
Answer: For the sake of avoiding grimdark I’ll say the existence of a political, competent Pokémon League got the ones who needed help the help they needed, while the others live happily.

Question: On Ash’s goal for Nidoking’s growth.
Answer: Yep, Ash desperately wants Nidoking to have his own identity. Pretty much the entire team invests a great deal of their identity into Ash - and he does the same for them - but Nidoking basically grew into an extension of Ash out of sheer loyalty.

Question: On the Black Broth.
Answer: Yep, I was inspired by the Spartan black soup. No exact recipe survived, but the main ingredients were pigs’ legs, blood, salt and vinegar. Obviously they aren’t using Pokémon legs for the broth (anymore), but I imagine protein from the artificial meat plants and a disgusting amount of nutrients are packed into it, plus a few secret ingredients for fun :slight_smile: I can’t spill all the secrets, but that’s the base!

Question: On Metagross’ diet.
Answer: Yep, I always figured that Metagross were ultimate omnivores. Probably a large amount of their diet comes from rock and steel-types, but they eat basically any living thing they can find in the wild.

Question: What would you say is the most dangerous Pokemon? Not strongest but just so insanely dangerous to train.
Answer: Honestly, Chandelure (and ghosts in general) are way up on that list.

Question: What does being a ghost type mean in Traveler?
Answer: Hmm, I’d say the closest I can get to a real definition (without too many spoilers) is an extradimensional entity that’s slipped into the material world. They’re eroded by existing in the Pokémon World, and require some corporeal form to possess and manipulate to continue existing.

Question: How do abilities like No Guard, Contrary, Protean, Wonderguard, etc. carry over to Traveler?
Answer: Pretty much as [redacted] described [see question 269]! Protean is something I have plans to address, but haven’t settled on a specific explanation. Contrary would probably be a more psychological thing - say a Pokémon tried to intimidate a Pokémon, but it would just infuriate the other Pokémon and make it more aggressive instead. No Guard is more psychological!

Question: Can some ghosts like Decidueye and Jellicent have eggs?
Answer: Yep, some ghosts can! It mostly depends on the species.

Question: What's the current listed height for Bruiser? I remember Bruno's Machamp being something like 6' and being referred to as massive for the species.
Answer: Gonna be honest, Bruno’s Machamp’s size will probably come down to being early installment weirdness! I’ve trended towards Pokémon being significantly larger than the PokeDex lists in most cases. Can’t remember if I’ve given an actual height for Bruiser in the past, but I’d say he’s somewhere around six feet? Bruno’s will probably suspiciously grow the next time he appears...although I do like the idea of a strangely short Machamp that’s just an absolute unit.

Question: How many vines does Tangrowth have roughly?
Answer: I’d say he has several hundred, but not all are suitable for combat. Many are thinner and designed for more delicate work, but he has dozens of combat ready vines that can extend to long ranges.

Question: If he[Tangrowth] had all of his combat one’s outstretched, would we see smaller vines protecting the rest of him? [see question 337]
Answer: Yep! Sort of like an undercoat of vines.  They’re responsible for a lot of his shock absorption.

Question: How do friendship evolution’s work in Traveler?
Answer: I can’t find the quote I gave [see question 118], but basically it tends to affect highly social Pokémon. When they have high positive feelings - which in the wild correlated to feelings of safety or status - they would evolve and assume leadership positions.

Question: If Ash were to channel Lightning through the Feather, would he be able to stimulate the evolution of any electric types?
Answer: Yep, he could. It would have to be directed into the electric-type (not just in the area) but would be able to stimulate evolution. It would be a struggle to mimic the intensity of an Electrizer, but would be feasible if he was careful.

Question: Is there a distinction between vertebrates and invertebrates in the Travelerverse? I meant neurologically.
Answer: I’m rolling with the ‘every Pokémon is sentient[sapient]’ category for Traveler purposes. It makes the wild and Pokémon training an ethical nightmare when looked at too closely, but it rolls with the suspension of disbelief. Some Pokémon have different thought patterns and might not have the same attachment and ‘human’ emotions that many Pokémon do, but can still think and operate at a much higher level than any animal in our world. Even the dumbest Pokémon tend to be very intelligent - I’d rank them at worst as dolphin or elephant levels of intelligence, but with psychic powers making inter species communication possible I think there’s a much higher awareness and value placed on them.

Question: I am a little surprised to not see more fire pokemon in Ash's team since he is in possession of the feather, I kind of thought they might get attracted to it a bit.
Answer: Honesty, Ash is a beacon for a lot of Pokémon at this point bht[but] is so mobile that they have a hard time keeping up. Pay attention to the references to Absol migratory movements while he was in Hoenn...

Question: On Ash’s growth.
Answer: Yep, honestly Ash is in the sweet spot where he has the motivation, is insanely dedicated to training at all hours, and found his way into the connections to maximize his talent. Not even mentioning that he’s pretty much addicted to battling lol. Oh, and the fact that his team freaking loves him. That helps a lot too.

Question: What sort of psychic niche does Grumpig fit into? Is it more mental technique oriented or physical manifestation focused?
Answer: It strikes a bit of a balance. Grumpig excels at defensive psychic abilities and limited physical manipulation, but also has a great deal of talent in striking mentally at opponents to daze or disorient them.

Question: How is its [Grumpig’s] teleportation? [see question 344]
Answer: Very limited, probably even more so than Dazed. It can move itself short distances but struggles in that aspect...exceptions exist, of course!

Question: How does the league properly protect their spectators for champion level ghost, dark, and bug special attacks? A shed load of Alakazam?
Answer: They also have specialized ghosts and dark/types capable of providing security for techniques that Alakazam just aren’t equipped to handle.

Question: Why is it that Alakazam have largely been used for barriers so far? being more common and or versatile?
Answer: Mostly they are versatile. They’re also very adept at keeping an eye on combatants thoughts and using that to prepare the defenses accordingly. Even when other psychics are used for additional power and security, Alakazam tend to be at the center of the network.

Question: On the fail safes in place during a Pokemon Battle.
Answer: There tends to be redundant failsafes, and it’s ridiculously difficult to actually shatter psychic barriers. It’s happened, but there is never only one layer protecting spectators in high-level matches. Bad PR if a bunch of people get burnt by an angry Magmortar.

Question: Is Brusier the biggest fucking tank of a Machoke in Traveler verse or what?
Answer: Yep, it’s even something that’ll be addressed when Ash meets Bruno haha. Rampage is essentially one of the only ways for a Machoke to reasonably increase their physical strength. Most Machoke are so ridiculously strong already that they would never try to increase it for fear of losing control - the way they see it, their strength is already 15/10 so it’s best to focus on other aspects of battling. So as a result Bruiser is one the strongest, most durable Machoke on the freaking planet...but it also ties into his fears of being unable to control that strength.

Question: On Ash’s team's natural ability.
Answer: Yep, Infernus is pretty much the only one of the teams that’s a natural monster. The others’ bodies have just adapted to the crazy training and dedication.

Question: On Ash’s team's future growth.
Answer: Oh yeah! We’re going to see some rapid growth when he [Trangrowth] gets to Hoenn and the pace picks up, and Ash will be getting that boost that lets him fight more fairly with Masters. It’ll open up a lot of new opponents for him and I can really have him go wild!

Question: Elf who’s your favorite Pokémon that you wrote?
Answer: Hmm, Dazed is the funnest to write in terms of dialogue and interactions - she has that extra layer through her telepathy.

Question: On how Infernus is regarded by other Pokemon Trainers.
Answer: One thing that helps Infernus is that he’s so terrifying to most trainers that EVERYONE is trying to think up counters for him.

Question: Do the public even know that the First Champion having a Rhydon is the reason for all the Rhydon symbols? Or has that been lost to time and just taken for granted in the present?
Answer: They do know that the First’s cornerstone was a Rhydon, and historians are aware he had a Ninetales and Tauros as well.

Question: Will we find out about what happened to Tangrowth before Ash caught him? And does destiny bond affect Infernus’s passive flames since it’s technically accidental damage?
Answer: Most likely not for Tangrowth, I want some things to stay mysteries! And yes, the feedback from the pain caused by Infernus’ flames would still affect Destiny Bond. Some Destiny Bond users will deliberately injure themselves to hurt their linked opponent.

Question: How does Distortion interact with Aura, if at all?
Answer: I won’t go into detail, but I will say that you can look at Aura as positive, psychic powers as neutral, and Distortion as negative and make of that what you will.

Question: How (if at all) does the Scyther > Scizor evolution happen in the wild? Or is Scizor only typically found in the hands of trainers/as released Pokemon? Is Pain Split similar to Destiny Bond in Traveler?
Answer: So my current theory for Scyther is something along the lines of their evolution taking place once they’ve hit their prime. Their armor slowly begins to harden, and at a certain point when they’ve reached full maturity their body registers higher level of minerals, which provides the impetus for evolution.

Question: In that respect then, I imagine Metal Coats are made to quicken up that process[evolution] for trainer owned Scythers and Onix, as well as just generally being useful for Steel and Rock types, I assume? [see question 357]
Answer: Yep! I figure they’re just a way of giving some control to the trainer and Pokémon instead of waiting for nature to take its course.

Question: I wonder how far back the historical record goes? Like we know there are entities like Chinatsu that tell his feats to others, but how much does the average person know about the time?
Answer: There are Pokémon that serve as living repositories of information as well as many who have destroyed vast amounts of information through their inherent power. Mamoru and Chinatsu singlehandedly set Fuchsia’s development back centuries in their rampage, for example. There are several examples of this that will be explored within the story!

Question: Has Mewtwo made plans to fight the other legends like Darkrai if it came to it?
Answer: After Mew’s warning Mewtwo has been very paranoid and is trying to seek others out. Witnessing the Unown and their power shook him badly.

Question: Is Ash going to have to participate in the Indigo League again and win it before challenging the Elite 4?
Answer: Nope, as a member of the League Ash will be able to make his own challenge when he’s ready.

Question: Will Ash be changing his tactics/strategy now that he battled Karen? Would Steven teachings sink in and Ash know when to use Lance powerhouse techniques properly?
Answer: Yep! Ash will be adapting due to his loss.

Question: Have you ever been afraid that you've made Ash's team a bit too powerful? If so, how did you remedy this?
Answer: Oh absolutely! If I could go back to 2012 I’d slow down his early development a bit. We’re seeing the struggle right now as he’s in the difficult spot of being too powerful for 99.9% of trainers to beat but isn’t quite ready to take down the best of the best. Finding compelling opponents for him is very difficult, and as a result Ash is generally using his weaker members more and more often to get a bit of a thof rill. But if it’s a real fight and he has to bust out Infernus or Torrent there’s very, very few cases where he could reasonably lose. It’s one of the reasons I’ve tried giving him new hobbies and interests outside training. It does give me an excuse to go full steam ahead into worldbuilding, though!

Question: When it comes to fleshing out Ash, how do you approach that?
Answer: Of course! So basically I worked it in as naturally as I could. In Pallet I figure most of their trainer classes would revolve around survival, practical skills, and science. With history somewhat neglected, I figure Ash’s original interest would mostly be shown through his love of his mom’s stories and myths.

Question: Does Traveler contain any small insects?
Answer: So it’s something I’m hesitant to give actual answers on, but here are my thoughts: bacteria and microorganisms exist 100%. They’re in the best position to survive extreme environments in the Pokémon World. Actual animals do not exist because they’d just be horrifically outcompeted on every front - intelligence, not being able to breathe fire, durability, etc.

Question: On whether Pokemon meat is sold.
Answer: There is a market for ‘real’ Pokémon meat. Super illegal but you know Team Rocket is in it with things like Slowpoke tails. Messed up rich people have to get their jollies somehow I guess.

Question: Are there pokemon that eat other pokemon much in the wild?
Answer: Most are primarily satisfied by League feeding stations, but it’s pretty common for hunting to occur, especially in the wild areas with fewer resources from the League. Prey Pokémon are generally accepting of occasional deaths from predators, but if a wild Pokémon is killing for the sake of killing the local populations will band together and kill the offender. There’s a balance there.

Question: I presume they’ve [Team Aqua] got a strong underground PR network and are seen as a more benevolent, covert Greenpeace? [see question 368]
Answer: Pretty much! They’re extremely popular and have no end of recruits. They also tend to be more powerful individually than Magma, although Magma has more resources and more powerful fighters due to heavy use of TMs and other training techniques.

Question: On Mewtwo’s willingness to battle.
Answer: Mewtwo’s still a bit of a bully. He doesn’t like fighting battles he’s not sure he can win, and the Unown were strange enough that he decided to stay out of it haha. Like you said [redacted], can’t blame him. He’s happy to just do his part in keeping Ash alive and letting him do the heavy lifting since Ash keeps ending up in these situations.

Question: Could Team Aqua be truly considered a criminal organization? considering they mostly do anti-poaching operations and help protect the archipelago.
Answer: That’s something I’ve mentioned before somewhere in Traveler. They’re into shady stuff, but the League largely tolerates their activity and works with them on occasion. Aqua is tolerated for helping protect the archipelago. Magma is more obviously sketchy but has powerful friends.

Question: If a shiny Metagross reproduces, would the Beldum also be shiny?
Answer: Yep, Shiny Metagross would produce new Shiny Beldum.

Question: If Metagross evolve by first Beldum combining into Metang, and then two metang into metagross, would that not lead to odd combinations if you had a shiny and a normal one? They're different metal compositions, no? Which gets precedence?
Answer: I can kind of imagine it as them basically pooling their different alloys during evolution, then redistributing it evenly over the Metang or Metagross. This could lead to degrees of shininess depending on how many of Metagross’ component Beldum were born shiny.

Question: Were all fossil pokemon rock types pre-fossilization?
Answer: It’s more of a trend than anything. Pokémon from those eras tend to be more primal, like Lance’s Aerodactyl. It’s not a hard rule, however, and they do have personalities beyond that.

Question: On Elf’s opinion about future Regions and their Pokemon.
Answer: Ha, I figure I can at least have them exist. Not sure if Ash will end up visiting them all except briefly, but I want to keep the world growing!

Question: On leftover space for future Regions in the Traveler World Map.
Answer: I tried to leave myself some safe space by creating several blank land masses for the map lol. Some I have plans for but others (plus the entire undiscovered part of the Southern Continent) are just there to slap the new regions onto.

Question: Would Ash getting a Fairy Type have some meaning or help him grow as Pokemon Trainer?
Answer: Yep, it would definitely provide some growth and new tactical choices.

Question: Does Magic, the one that turned Ash into Ashachu, exist in the Traveller verse?
Answer: Hmm, not really. The closest you could get is Aura which has an essentially unlimited versatility, but which is entirely dependent on the individual. Then again, once you get to the Legendary psychics like Mewtwo they can essentially do whatever they want, so that might as well count as magic.

Question: I swear if I remember right didn't a Lugia get turned Into Shadow Pokemon XD001 right?
Answer: For the earlier discussion, I’ll just say and confirm that XD001 was not Lugia but maintained the same level of power we saw in the game - pulling the cruise ship from the ocean and dumping it in the desert happened, for example.

Question: On Cipher.
Answer: Yep, Cipher solves the biggest problem in war: having soldiers willing to kill. Most Pokémon aren’t willing to mercilessly slaughter or follow ruthless orders without a good cause, especially without a real connection with their trainer. Cipher created fighting machines and used them to great effect.

Question: Do Mismagius within Traveler also subsist off of fear in a similar way to Dazed with dreams? If so, would Mismagius literally just attack with Confuse Ray against potential prey in order to feed?
Answer: Yep! They feed off emotions. We’ll get to learn a bit more next chapter! But I have it as most ghosts being able to feed off emotions in general, but it’s a hundred times easier to jump out and scare a victim to feed than it is to make them happy and feed off that. But yes, Mismagius might use weak techniques to enhance their victim’s fear, but most try to be delicate. Going full out on squishy humans is a good way to get Karen and her SPECTERs on their trail.

Question: I’m kind of curious if Mismagius would need to migrate from town to town in order to prevent desensitisation from starving them?
Answer: Yep! In rural areas or small towns it’s pretty common for Mismagius and other ghosts to need to move around frequently. In larger towns there’s enough prey that they don’t have to worry about it, but they do have to take a little less from each victim so it doesn’t turn into a big enough issue to attract attention.

Question: Would Mewtwo actually live for Millenia, or potentially even tens of thousands of years?
Answer: Naturally, I think Mewtwo would have either a human or slightly shorter life span since Team Rocket’s genetic engineering was so cutting edge - they hadn’t had time to really perfect it.

Question: What would you say is your favorite created technique?
Answer: Hmm my favorite one that I’ve shown so far is probably Rampage! My real favorite hasn’t been revealed yet.

Question: On Suicune’s strength.
Answer: Suicune just embodies a different concept than Entei or Raikou but they’re all the same level of strength.

Question: If a Concept like Suicune embodies is irrevocably changed, what happens?
Answer: Hmm interesting question! I don’t think they can be irrevocably changed. You can see it nudged in a certain direction or diluted by another Concept temporarily, but I think it would always return to its baseline given time.

Question: So Suicune just was concerned about accidental collateral damage, then, and temporarily altering its concept? [see question 386]
Answer: Yep, and more than anything concerned about irrevocably damaging Ash. He’s not as easy to change as most humans, but he’s still pretty squishy at this point.

Question: How aware are the other Leagues about Ash's involvement in the Legendaries event? I imagine the National League knows a lot, but how about Unova and Kalos'?
Answer: National League is fully aware. They’re privy to every bit of information Ash has told Lance and the League. The National League has ensured the other Leagues are aware of the threat and provided a huge amount of scientific data supporting their claims, but leave Ash out of it as much as possible.

Question: Does anyone know how old Ash was at the Conference Finals?
Answer: He turned eleven in Chapter 3 on the way to Vermillion I believe. He turned 12 in Chapter 32, I think. Right before he went to Hoenn.

Question: The Sinnohoans go by a different calendar?
Answer: Sinnoans had their own calendar based on the beginning of the Alfa dynasty, but it’s fallen out of use. Pretty much the whole world has adapted to PIL as a result of the National League being such a monolithic entity.

Question: On a future Bruiser v. Snorlax rematch.
Answer: Rampage!Bruiser in a rematch against Snorlax would be really, really cool. Snorlax would be able to survive multiple hits pretty easily, but Bruiser would also be in the situation of completely outclassing Snorlax in maneuverability. Plus Bruiser’s still in the situation where he doesn’t get superior anchoring or anything - hits from Snorlax would send him flying.

Question: How replicable is Air Lens?
Answer: Air Lens is a fairly simple technique for certain species like Magmar and Magcargo, but extremely draining for other fire-types. They can learn it but it can be best used in short bursts instead of sustained.

Question: How strong Bruiser really is?
Answer: I don’t honestly have the expertise to give exact numbers! What I will say is that Bruiser is to the point where increases in raw strength don’t actually matter much in battle - his raw strength is so much above other fighting-types that it doesn’t matter what he’s facing other than another super strong Pokémon. At this point his biggest issues are anchoring and getting leverage to actually use his ridiculous strength to its full potential.

Question: When he [Bruiser] uses Rock Tomb, would he use that to give himself better leverage and to unbalance his opponents? If fighting another super strong Pokémon? [see question 393]
Answer: Yep, Rock Tomb will be great for bracing himself and keeping him in a fight. Protect as well.

Question: On Oz’s stamina as an Electivire.
Answer: Oz will have better stamina once she evolves, but Infernus is just a natural stamina freak...partly because he throws 100% into every battle. She can improve, but I don’t think she’ll ever quite match him on that front.

Question: On the effect of Rampage on Bruiser’s growth.
Answer: Rampage basically counted as an evolution for him haha. It compensated for so many of his weaknesses.

Question: How does the energy used by Dragons work, is it produced in an organ like Dark types have for Distortion?
Answer: Good question! So, I have some thoughts on draconic energy but I’m still working on some aspects. I won’t go into detail yet but I will give you some tentative basics.

Question: It's especially interesting that you mentioned Draconic Energy being latent through the universe, since (and IIRC you did play OR) so is Infinity Energy. Is it then the case that those are one and the same? [see question 397]
Answer: Infinity Energy is difficult. It gives me just enough that I can make some guesses and try to work it into my framework, but it’s a little frustrating that they have such little information on it in Gen VI and then proceed to never revisit it.

Question: On Ash’s team longevity. [see question 46] [see question 130]
Answer: Torrent will most likely live for around two centuries. Tangrowth and Aron are biologically immortal. The rest are somewhere around human length.

Question: Do all dragons have that sort of longevity, or is it limited to the Kingdra line? [see question 399]
Answer: Dragonite can live up to two hundred. Elder Yari’s Dragonite just sustained massive damage in the Last War that cut her lifespan down dramatically.

Question: What happens to pokemon that outlive their trainer, like Torrent when Ash kicks the bucket?
Answer: Some choose to work with the League or remain with their trainer’s friends or families. Others go their own way and live out in the wild - these are often the ones the League likes to keep as contacts in the wild Pokémon community.

Question: Are psychic powers fueled by emotion, as well as the mind? Would a happy memory like ponded infernus be a fuel to make  psychics more powerful?
Answer: Sort of! It’s a bit of a double-edged Sword. Emotions like anger can help provide brief spurts of focus - or let psychic output immense amounts of force as they ignore their own limits - but it also poses the risk of breaking their focus, which is instrumental in using psychic abilities. That brief surge of power is like running a sprint - a huge, quick output but a psychic will be exhausted afterward.

Question: When champions spar, like Lance and Steven, do they use mega’s? Because it was stated that it took either 3-4 of Lances Pokémon to take down metagross, but is that normal meta or mega meta?
Answer: Mega are only very rarely used in sparring! It happens, but the strength it gives a Champion-level pokemon makes them extremely dangerous. That kind of strength can’t be easily controlled.

Question: On Cynthia vs Steven. [see question 221]
Answer: Cynthia and Steven’s fights can go either way. Steven kind of hard counters her by forcing her to expend two or three of her valuable teammates just to bring down Metagross, but she’s great at outmaneuvering the others...not that they aren’t extremely powerful themselves.

Question: What would you say is the difference between steel types' armour?
Answer: Basically their genes just result in the production of different alloys in their armor. Diet and environment also plays a part.

Question: Hasn’t Spiritomb also been mentioned to gradually getting weaker or calmer overtime by Cynthia?
Answer: Yep, Spiritomb is gradually growing weaker as it loses the hatred and malice that fuel it and replaces it with more positive emotions. That said, it can learn to use the new abilities it gains with the new emotions if it’s creative enough...which, with Cynthia as its trainer, it surely is.

Question: On Metagross vs. Tyranitar.
Answer: As for Metagross vs. Tyranitar, that honestly comes down to training. In the wild, a lone average Metagross would lose to an average Tyranitar...but they would also probably avoid each other since nothing has anything to gain. Plus a Metagross is likely to have an entire colony backing them up.

Question: What is the justification for poison types being weak to psychics?
Answer: I think I referred to it somewhere in the Conference maybe? Basically psychics are extremely good at pinpointing vulnerabilities and poison sacs with their abilities, which are extremely damaging if ruptured.

Question: How soon after the Conference did Greenfield happen?
Answer: Roughly three months after the Closing Ceremony!

Question: How much bloodier was Orre in Traveler during the events of [Pokemon] XD?
Answer: It was definitely at its worst in Colosseum. Wes brought a measure of peace to the region, but it was really just a breath of fresh air. XD was pretty bad, but things only went to Colosseum levels when Michael left [see question 223].

Question: Do we know how the other regional champions in Traveler compare to the big three [Cynthia, Lance and Steven ]?
Answer: I’ll have to keep it a little briefer than I’d like, but I’ll be able to go into more detail this afternoon if you’d like!

Question: And Metagross are very statistical in nature, right? So how would they fare in this [a card game called Mao]?
Answer: I think Metagross would be great at the statistical part - probably the best blackjack player of all time - but Metagross would struggle in something like Mao where there are unwritten rules. They can logic it out, but they’re fairly inflexible. Alakazam would be amazing though.

Question: The main reason I was curious structure wise was that it was stated that Drake got 3 E4 kills during the Last War, for clarification. [See question 411]
Answer: That's honestly just an inconsistency since I hadn’t developed aspects of the Last War’s repercussions, but one way I might explain it in-story is that Unova and Kalos had already adapted a powerful core of Masters directly serving the government before the Last War. It’s easy to imagine the National League just translating that as ‘Oh, it’s the Unovan Elite Four!”.

Question: Why Alakazam? The reading minds thing? [see question 412]
Answer: Yep, Alakazam and Metagross are both hyper intelligent and capable of processing vast amounts of raw information, but they specialize in different areas.

Question: Honestly in direct battle, I'd imagine Gallade's probably scarier than both [Alakazam and Metagross].
Answer: A properly trained Gallade is terrifying, especially in a larger scale battle where they can abuse teleportation under the cover of the battlefield’s chaos. They’re amazing at going after squishier psychics that tend to avoid the frontlines.

Question: Beyond teleportation, would a Gallade be more of a physical psychic specialist?
Answer: Yep. Gallade are fairly powerful naturally, so they’d be able to use some mental effects but not to as much effect without proper training. They are excellent at mind-reading, however. While they can use ranged attacks, I view their optimal strategies as teleporting to their opponent and overwhelming them with offense (ideally) or, if they’re incapable of teleportation, using their psychic abilities to protect themselves from ranged attacks and increase their speed while they attempt to close the gap.

Question: When it comes to a Champion being recognised as a Master, is there a criteria that must be met in order for a committee or organisation of some kind to recognise you?
Answer: No committee or organization involved in being recognized as a Master! It’s typically just when you’ve achieved enough or shown Master-level skill. A common time to be recognized a Master is defeating or fighting an Elite Four member (or other recognized Master) on fairly even terms. A lot of it just acknowledgement of your skills by another Master.

Question: Is it fine for us to know the relationship between Alder and the other champions?
Answer: Sure! Alder is on pretty great terms with everyone. His rebellion and coup made the invasion far less bloody than it might’ve been so the National League likes him. The former Champion and leaders of Kalos weren’t huge fans, but he and Diantha are on good terms and rekindled the friendship between their regions.

Question: Does Professor Oak get included in the same channels as Steven Stone when they're both ex-champions? Or is Steven a special case due to his involvement with Pierce? Is Oak a special exception as he's turned to a researching career, and thus would occupy a different position within the league with his services?
Answer: So, basically Oak and Steven are in similar boats where they’ve maintained extremely close ties with the League. Oak has his own responsibilities, but still works closely with them on essential, confidential projects such as SCRY Project. Steven takes a more direct role in acting as a field agent of the League.

Question: I take it that Ash is recognized as a Master or on the verge of being recognized as one?
Answer: Yep! Ash is basically just beneath proper Master-level. He just needs some refinement. A more established Master like Koga or Bruno would bulldoze him (and by that I mean probably 3 or 4 to 6 in a match) but less experienced Masters like Will and Karen are in his weight class.

Question: Would Infernus technically be considered Ash's Ace?
Answer: Yep, I’d definitely consider Infernus as the cornerstone. Aside from being a combat beast, he’s instrumental in setting up the battlefield for other teammates.

Question: What's the Champion induction process like?
Answer: There is a Hall of Fame that only Champions are allowed into where they are inducted, along with a formal ceremony witnessed by the public. Generally, new Champions are trained by their predecessor in the role for a year or two, upon which the previous Champion formally steps down.

Question: Actually is Ash his [Lance’s] first student/apprentice?
Answer: Yep, Ash is his first real student. Lance helps other members of the Elite Four; but his specialty hasn’t lined up with others. Ash is his first dedicated student.

Question: What new regulations do the Leagues are putting on Unown? Like, can you now never gather more than 100 in one place or something?
Answer: Yep, outside of one or two odd individuals that are found they’re extremely uncommon! Most are only found in protected archaeological sites, and oftentimes trainers aren’t very interested in the rare Unown they do find other than as a curiosity. They aren’t the best battlers on their own!

Question: On human development.
Answer: For Traveler, humans are basically identical to real world humans. If I was redoing Traveler, I would age Ash up a few years. At least 13 or 14 (still incredibly young) before going on his journey. But trainers are exposed to circumstances that tend to force them to mature faster.

Question: Would prolonged contact with psychic Pokemon also accelerate mental maturity?
Answer: Depends on the psychic Pokémon! What is certain is that it will change you in some ways, even if it doesn’t necessarily make you mature.

Question: I wonder if humans would have a weakened ability to communicate verbally? [see question 426]
Answer: Yep, there’s a ton of factors [like age of the human when exposed, length and type of exposure, strength and intelligence of the psychic Pokemon in question, etc.] at play! And that’s an interesting question. I think the child would quickly adapt to primarily communicating with their thoughts, even using their thoughts to ask questions. Their ability to speak verbally would atrophy quickly. It does depend on whether the child is psychic or not.

Question: Can the Magnemite line be artificially created in Traveller?
Answer: Nope, they’re completely natural! I do imagine them looking slightly different in Traveler - completely identical, but they appear a bit more natural and rugged instead of the wholly artificial look.

Question: On the Infernus clone created by Mewtwo.
Answer: Fun fact: There is an Infernus clone, Mewtwo just thought that their fight would cause too much collateral damage.

Question: How do porygon work? Can they battle since we have seen them as mostly data, and how were they discovered?
