Answer: They can battle if uploaded into a physical form, but their greatest utility is definitely outside of combat. They were created fairly recently, but are useful enough to see widespread adoption. [see question 274]

Question: Elf, favorite and least favorite to write? [see question 352]
Answer: I have a few favorites (other than Ash of course). I’ll only use those that have dialogue for this.

Question: Do we know anything about Fergus, Corey and Neesha’s families?
Answer: I haven’t added anything about their families to Traveler, anyways.

Question: On a potential Neesha vs. Grey battle.
Answer: For that, it’s another of those situations where Grey is seriously hampered in regulated matches, while Neesha is at her strongest in them.

Question: I imagine the only one that can really fight at their fullest in them is [Grey’s] Scolipede? [see question 433]
Answer: Yep, Scolipede is a bit of an exception in that it’s an assassin, but even it’s hurt since it prefers to operate under the cover of battlefield chaos.

Question: On Oak’s Charizard.
Answer: I will say that Oak’s Charizard is WAY up there. He’s never stopped training. It’s getting older, but it could 100% keep up with Magnus.

Question: Based on that tier list and Elf's statement that Flint's magmortar would beat Infernus quite comfortably, does that mean that Flint's magmortar is Lance's Dragonite or Cynthia's Garchomp level?
Answer: It’s a weird situation. Infernus with teleport is probably better able to take on certain Champion-level opponents, but Flint’s Magmortar has WAY more experience taking on Fire-types and other Magmortar. Plus it’s genuine Champion-level. I think Cynthia’s worked out too many counters to it for it to be a challenge anymore.

Question: I'm surprised infernus hasn't tried to fight Oak's Charizard, and only tried going for arcanine. Did he ever beat Arcanine?
Answer: Charizard hasn’t had any interest in fighting Infernus, and ranges too far from Pallet for Infernus to track him down. Infernus hasn’t beaten Arcanine yet, but he’s come close.

Question: On Infernus’ potential parenthood.
Answer: Nah, but for real I actually do imagine Infernus being a surprisingly good dad. Mostly because he wants a strong challenger in a few years.

Question: Does Steven swear or does he just have a filter around Ash?
Answer: Steven doesn’t swear gratuitously, but it happens. He definitely censors himself around Ash though!

Question: What is the order of Prof in terms of power level? I think it's Oak=Rowan>Birch and Elm?
RelatedTags: ['Champion', 'Pokemon Battle', 'Pokemon Trainer', 'Professor Birch', 'Professor Elm', 'Professor Oak', 'Professor Rowan']
Answer: I don’t see Rowan as a real trainer, but I’m sure he has Pokémon companions and partners! Plus he could always intimidate any Pokémon. He doesn’t need to be a Champion like Oak.

Question: Does Hydreigon actually have intelligence in its’ other heads? Or are they just other sets of eyes and when they snap at each other it’s because Hydreigon is so stupid that it doesn’t realise that it’s fighting itself there?
Answer: I imagine them to be semi-intelligent. They don’t have proper brains, but have well-developed enough nervous systems that they’re able to react to stimulus. They also receive sensory information from the main head that helps guide them.

Question: What did you think of my hypothesis that the original leaders of Ecruteak were Bearers of Ho Ohs and Lugia's Feathers? The Tao and Tsuki?
Answer: That was a great insight! The Taiyo and Tsuki are descended from Twin Champions of Ho-Oh and Lugia. They had true authority and power, and their descendants inherited a close connection to Lugia and Ho-Oh. Over time the legends grew muddled, partly due to the Taiyo and Tsuki intentionally spreading propaganda that painted them as true descendants of the Legends to maintain their authority.

Question: Does Metagross have any scarring that you can think of?
Answer: I imagine it to have several scratches across its chassis, but it’s so effective at this point that most have darkened to just a shade lighter than its normal color. I can imagine it having a claw mark left over from his battle with Drake that’s still noticeable.

Question: Did Grey have the organ surgically removed or did he remove it in a more savage (for lack of a better word) way? And was the removal consensual and/or legal?
Answer: So I’ll answer the biggest questions first: It was legal and Hydreigon consented to the surgery. It’s an uncommon procedure, but not unheard of in League teams such as ACE trainers if their team isn’t specifically focused on Distortional abilities and the Pokémon isn’t too reliant on that aspect of their abilities.

Question: On the hurdles Torrent must overcome when battling Infernus.
Answer: Teleport is also a nightmare for Torrent. It lets Infernus close the distance immediately and get past Ice Storm if Infernus manages to force Torrent out of the water.

Question: Do you limit yourself to the learnsets of Pokémon in game, or if it’s feasible for them to learn the move then they can?
Answer: Yep. I stick as close to the games as possible, but I can be flexible. Plus if something’s just an extension of their current (or learnable) abilities they can generally learn it.

Question: How does Wallace compare to the champions we have seen. Did Steven abdicate from his position and Wallace just was the strongest person who was willing to take the job or did Wallace take it from Steven?
Answer: Wallace is a true Master. He specializes in the sheer complexity of techniques. He can pull things off intricate enough that Ash will have a hard time believing they’re possible. [see question 198]

Question: I'm curious how Elf is going to handle the Gen 8 and the awful decision to do Legendary [Birds] Regional variants.
Answer: I won’t be spending much time on later regions, but I do have plans for the variants!

Question: Have there been any attempts in infusing Pokémon DNA into Humans in the Traveller to make Pokémon/Human hybrids? Or is this something that's not to be known yet?
Answer: Not particularly. It’s not something the League would condone. I’m 100% confident that Giovanni would have had experiments going for it, but I doubt they would meet much success. His experiments were more focused on Mewtwo (which provided all sorts of useful information for other genetic enhancement experiments) and otherwise creating enhanced Pokémon.

Question: Do they [the League] use actual cash like bills and coins? Or is it more of a bitcoin kind of thing, where people can’t see the money?
Answer: The League has many, many sources of income. Taxation is the major one - I have a hard time imagining Leagues directly taxing property and residents in their territory, but what I do imagine is taxing battle wagers and other trainer activities while their primary source of income is paid by cities to the League from their tax revenue. While the League is a massive overarching presence, they tend to allow cities to handle things like taxation themselves so long as the rules are followed.

Question: Are pokedollars based on the gold or silver standard or is it something like fiat money?
Answer: I’d say fiat.

Question: On the League’s social programs.
Answer: I do imagine the League tends to have very strong social programs. Pokémon make it very easy for many basic needs to be filled.

Question: On Ash’s economic status.
Answer: And yep, Ash is very well off. The League likes to keep their elite trainers well taken care of!

Question: I guess that Team Rocket was also a major rejection of that communal kind of society with their focus on profit and accumulating power?
Answer: And yep, I see Team Rocket as being part of a new wave. The world isn’t quite to that point yet, but as technology advances rapidly and reliance on Pokémon and traditional systems becomes (slightly) less necessary, there are people who are taking a much more human-centric view.

Question: What’re we thinking about the cost of Mewtwo?
Answer: Yep, I imagine the Mewtwo project went easily into the billions. Team Rocket and their benefactors have deep, deep pockets. It doesn’t hurt that the technology and discoveries developed during the project could also be used in other ventures and repurposed for commercial use.

Question: Is it confirmed that Ditto were the result of Rocket experimentation with Mew DNA?
Answer: I haven’t confirmed it in-story, but Ditto are failed attempts at Mewtwo. Team Rocket didn’t realize their potential at first and many excess specimens escaped or were released.

Question: Can Dittos copy legendaries?
Answer: Nope, Ditto aren’t capable of copying Legends...it doesn’t help that only a tiny handful of Legendaries actually have DNA.

Question: How much does winning a Contest and Grand Festival earn? Same as A gym Battle and League?
Answer: I definitely imagine winning the Grand Festival to be similar to ranking highly in the Conferences in terms of prizes - it’s a nice payday. The real money definitely comes from stuff like sponsorships, though.

Question: Can a League Winner or Runner up get a sponsorship?
Answer: Yep! I imagine there’s tons of people who’d love to get in contact with Ash for it lol.

Question: Where did the battles [during the Last War] take place? The cities?
Answer: Many battles took place on the coast or on the sea due to Unova’s fleets (and most major cities in the Pokémon World being based on the coast). Drake won his first major victory in a battle at Lilycove if I remember right.

Question: What would Celebi’s concept be anyway? I suppose Guardian of Time would make sense, seeing as Mew is the Guardian of Life.
Answer: Yep, you can kind of envision Guardian-type Legendaries such as Mew and Lugia to be along the lines of maintenance. Mew ensures the continuation of Life, for example, although it doesn’t directly embody it.

Question: Is it mandatory to take the elite 4 challenge if one wins a conference?
Answer: Nope. Most people at least attempt it but there’s nothing forcing you to!

Question: How does one get to be an Ace trainer?
Answer: ACE Trainers are generally recruited from Conference members! Generally the final few rounds - probably starting at Top 32 and maybe Top 64 for special cases - are where they’re drawn from. There’s extensive training for trainers in terms of law, tactics, and teamwork with similar training for their Pokémon.

Question: Do the E4 actually go all-out in the E4 challenges?
Answer: Yep. One thing to keep in mind is that the Elite Four and Champion are designed to be as stable as possible. Very few trainers can beat even a single Elite Four member and that’s how the League likes it - while the system is designed to reward hard work and talent, they would rather keep members on as long as possible.

Question: On Torrent’s strength.
Answer: Plus Torrent is just a monster by any regard. He gets overshadowed a bit by Infernus’ personality, but not many Pokémon want to be anywhere near Torrent in a fight.

Question: Does Mewtwo sleep?
Answer: Mewtwo doesn’t sleep. Biologically he needs it, but he has enough control over his body to refresh himself constantly.

Question: I want to ask about what Team Rocket is and how did it start? Was it inherited by family or by the Alpha there?
Answer: So, none of this is set in stone but this is the premise I’m currently working with.

Question: That was the First Rocket war? [see question 467]
Answer: Yep! It had started a few years before as the League began to crack down on the Rockets, but Giovanni is the one who ramped it up to the next level.

Question: If his [Giovanni’s] family is former nobles, would that be why he retains the Sakaki name? Similar to the Wataru in Johto?
Answer: And yep, that’s right [redacted].

Question: On the Champion line of succession.
Answer: Also, I caught up with the discussion and wanted to clear up a few things that I realize are a bit ambiguous in the text:

Question: Was the League back then aware of Giovanni involvement? [see question 467]
Answer: And nope, the League was never aware of Giovanni’s involvement. He kept his position by ruthlessly cracking down on Team Rocket and keeping Viridian the safest city in Kanto.

Question: On the crystal created by the Unown.
Answer: Yep. The crystal is essentially just the Unown’s stored psychic power. Their excess. It could theoretically envelop the world, although I think the Legends would put a stop to that quickly.

Question: On whether Legendaries can be caught with Master Balls.
Answer: Yep. And I imagine that Legendaries would be capable of easily breaking out of even Master Balls - and that’s if it even manages to reach them in the first place without being obliterated by the Legend’s aura. Even the Ultra Ball against Mewtwo was protected by Mew, if I remember right.

Question: What did Chinastu do with her original Pokeball?
Answer: She didn’t have one! She and Mamoru (technically Shinobu too) predate the invention of pokeballs. I can’t remember the exact timeline, but I don’t think Apricorn balls were widely available if they existed. Perhaps localized in specific areas in Johto at that point?

Question: On legendary moves.
Answer: I agree. Legendary techniques are something else, generally ridiculously complex in scale and scope. I think Clefable could use them, but it would take actual effort and training.

Question: Obviously days and months exist, but would they have the names we know?
Answer: I do use the normal month names for my own sanity, but I do try to avoid reference to them when at all possible, trying to use seasons mostly. Early summer, late winter, etc.

Question: Are there different languages in the Traveler's universe?
Answer: [See question 93] Also, the more I think about it the more I like the idea of Indigo and Hoenn having similar languages, but Sinnoh having developed somewhat differently due to its history of isolation.

Question: What do you think will be the first case of human intervention and experimentation on Pokémon?
Answer: Hmm I don’t know if I totally understand the question, so please correct me if I’m addressing the wrong thing! There’s actually been quite a bit of experimentation on Pokémon already - the League avoids harmful experimentation, but organizations like Team Rocket and pre-Last War Unova’s government are more than happy to experiment with chemicals and combat enhancers. I kind of imagine that’s where the root research of the X items came from.

Question: I noticed it [the timeline] said Will beat Bruno and Lorelei? Is that still canon?
Answer: As for the Will thing...that’s something I’ll probably end up soft retconning.

Question: Which will be the first non-legend and non-mythical Pokemon created?
Answer: I definitely imagine Geodude and Onix to be among the first - very basic, very tough, and able to survive in the harsh environments of the early world (and active Legendaries). Same for Pokémon that seem very primordial such as Magmar or Slugma. Kind of like sharks in that they’re very good at what they do and don’t change too much. And that’s just a few as examples - just the first to come to mind for me.

Question: On how a normal Pokemon can become as powerful as a Legendary.
Answer: Not going to touch on Jirachi, but I will say that the only way for a mortal Pokémon to compare to a Legend is to be uplifted by a Legend - Ho-Oh investing a huge amount of its essence into the Beasts is the best example for now - or to master something like one of the Elemental Spheres, which are essentially another Moltres/Articuno/Zapdos in another form. That’s...extremely impossible for about 99.99% of humans and Pokémon though. The unprepared mind would be obliterated.

Question: Can the legends... be killed?
Answer: I will say that if you somehow managed to destroy a Legend, it would basically instantly reform. They’re parts of the universe. Constant.

Question: Can a concept be absorbed?
Answer: Concepts can’t really be absorbed. Even with the Guardian-type Concepts you can almost think of them as side effects of the greater Concept’s existence.

Question: On whether Karen is a Master Trainer.
Answer: Karen actually does count as a Master, but she’s definitely on the lower end of the spectrum. Still very fresh.

Question: What is the SCRY Project?
Answer: We’ll learn more about it later, but it’s the project I’ve referred to in the past that the League is running in order to gather intel on the Legends.

Question: Are Pokémon Towers or Battle Frontiers or the Battle Chateau a thing in Traveler?
Answer: Sort of! There’s definitely battling resorts/facilities, but the Battle Frontier concept is still being put into action in Hoenn. It’s about a year off from opening.

Question: How close are Agatha and Surge?
Answer: I can’t really see them as friends - neither are much for that - but I imagine they’re friendly. Lots of mutual respect.

Question: Has Elf discussed Natures and how they work in Traveler?
Answer: Nature is basically just the Pokémon’s personality. It’s not really a thing, just tends to affect how they act in battle. More aggressive, more defensive and cautious, etc.

Question: On whether Metagross depends on sound.
Answer: Problem is that Metagross’ psychic awareness gives it an excellent idea of its general surroundings. It barely relies on sound.

Question: Could you expand a little bit more on the role of the League President?
RelatedTags: ['Champion', 'Charles Goodshow', 'Ever Grande', 'Indigo', 'Lily of the Valley', 'League', 'National League']
Answer: Charles Goodshow is the current National League President. This is largely a symbolic role, but does offer the President a role in dealing with foreign nations and treaties, although all of these have to be approved by the Champions of the Indigo, Ever Grande, and Lily of the Valley Leagues.

Question: What are the foreign nations?
Answer: Unova, Kalos, Alola, Galar, etc. The Ranger Union.

Question: On the Ranger Union.
Answer: Yep. The Ranger Union was originally a part of Sinnoh’s territory, essentially provinces conquered a long time ago. Kalos’ only military action in the war was to keep Sinnoh occupied, and they invaded the Ranger Union and unleashed tons of Kalosian wild Pokémon there. Sinnoh pulled back, largely abandoning the Ranger Union in order to consolidate its strength, and as a result the Ranger Union became its own entity.

Question: Where did Oak originate from?
Answer: As for Oak, I imagine his family was originally from Celadon but that Oak ended up in Viridian. I need to revisit my notes on that!

Question: Do the Indigo League deal with twice as many challengers, considering the Elite Four has to deal with the victors from the Indigo and Silver Conferences?
Answer: Yep, the Indigo League received challengers from both Conferences!

Question: Which amounts to two per year? [see question 494]
Answer: Yep! Although Conference Champions aren’t required to go through with challenging the Elite Four. Most do, though, if only to test their skills.

Question: Previous year challengers can come back, from what I remember?
Answer: Yep, they only have the right to challenge the Elite Four once. If they accept a position from the League, however, they might be given the right to challenge them again.

Question: What happens to most conference champions?
Answer: Most Conference Champions lose the Elite Four challenge (badly) and are offered into the ACE program or Elite Four trainee program. Generally the League recruits from the Top 64 for ACE.

Question: Karen is another Conference Champion, right?
Answer: Yes, Karen was a Conference Champion!

Question: Like full exhibition matches - do the Elite Four have full access to (presumably the Conference battlefields, as they're the most protected?) if they want to grapple with each other?
Answer: Yep, and I can see an Elite Four exhibition match in a Conference grabbing a lot of attention. Most people never get a chance to see that level of battling.

Question: On Blaine’s current strength.
Answer: Yep, Blaine has definitely fallen from his former heights. Several members of his team received major injuries in the war that led to lower life expectancy.

Question: Is there a traveler reason for why ghost types can't hit normal types and vice versa?
Answer: I’m still tinkering with my explanation for normal-types not being affected by ghost techniques, so I’ll pass on that for now. Ghost-types are able to ignore many physical attacks because they tend to have some control over their bodies that lets them twist or even dematerialize for a brief amount of time. Elemental attacks tend to be better because there’s something ‘extra’ that can linger or act as a sustained attack that ghosts can’t just phase through.

Question: What are the mechanics behind odor sleuth and Foresight?
Answer: Odor Sleuth I imagine is that Pokémon try to track a target’s scent. It doesn’t let them attack ghosts with normal-type or physical attacks, but it does let them keep an eye on them and know where they’re coming from so they can time attacks better to get past the ghost’s defenses.

Question: Does physical attacks also extend to stuff like Stone Edge for example, where they just phase through the physical material the move is composed of?
Answer: Yep! I’ve basically extended the weakness to all physical attacks. Stone Edge would be much harder to phase through if it was more of a stream. Fire is effective against ghosts because it’s more of a continuous stream in a lot of cases, and will still heat the air to a point where it can damage the ghost when it rematerializes.

Question: So would SPECTRE have normal type specialists as well?
Answer: Nope! SPECTRE is basically purely focused on Distortion.

Question: Do fire types have latent psychic abilities?
Answer: Yep! That’s how Pokémon are able to guide their flames to some degree, but most have little skill with it beyond the basics, and a lot of them use their latent abilities to help ignite the spark as well.

Question: Do you think it's possible to train against confuse ray, by repeated exposure?
Answer: As for Confuse Ray, I don’t think you can develop a resistance to it. There are techniques that will allow a Pokémon to have a resistance to it - such as flooding their body with Distortion to basically make them a temporary dark-type - but the Confuse Ray we see from specialists and Masters is just too potent to naturally develop a resistance to.

Question: On what classifies as a Pseudo Legendary.
Answer: Basically what I call pseudo-Legendary in Traveler is these monstrous Pokémon that are essentially demigods. The King, Clefable, etc. Just a fun twist of the normal term for me haha. [see question 91] [see question 227]

Question: On the strength of Pokemon being trained in the present in comparison to those trained in the past.
Answer: Yep! Especially when the power level of Pokémon tended lower. Even the strongest trained Pokémon of Taimu’s day would barely scratch Gym Leader now with access to more advanced training, nutrition, TMs, etc.

Question: On Pierce's strength.
Answer: Pierce is a bit unique in that he’s not so much strong as he is dangerous. His team doesn’t hesitate to kill which is a massive advantage in his favor, particularly with Metagross. Metagross is stronger than the rest of his team by a wide margin (I guess that quality runs in the family) and is a massive force amplifier that would let him take on most Gym Leaders evenly.

Question: On Ash’s view on death.
Answer: Yep! I think even now Ash would choose to accept death and not fight it, although he might be tempted if it meant he could stay with his team. He’s been in so many dangerous situations that he’s sort of internalized the prospect of his own death. He’s a bit numb to the idea, even if he doesn’t want to die. Desensitized.

Question: How much does the Metagross colony share its stored knowledge? Does the League consult them? Especially now with the Legendaries events happening?
Answer: Fairly often. Nothing earth shattering, but very basic information about the fall of the Volumo empire and what caused it has been gathered, and they also provide tracking of nearby Pokémon and inform the League of Rocket activity. That’s about it, though. The colony is close to the Stone family, but that only barely applies to the League.

Question: How do other trainers sort that out [exceeding the amount of Pokemon a Trainer can carry]?
Answer: There are both private and League facilities that care for Pokémon you aren’t able to have on you. The Corral is technically one of those facilities, although Oak restricts access to those he personally knows or trusts.

Question: How much did it cost to make Mewtwo? Like, could they have theoretically made an army of them (not that they would serve them but)? Also, could other Legends be cloned like Mew was, or would that be impossible due to lack of DNA?
Answer: Yep, I view the cost for the Mewtwo in the billions. Team Rocket was a very, very rich and powerful organization and Giovanni spared no expense. [Redacted] is correct in saying that Giovanni viewed Mewtwo as his magnum opus. His masterwork that would secure his position at the top of the world, ushering in his age.

Question: Could a normal trainer even afford feeding a Metagross?
Answer: And sort of? Metagross are extremely expensive to feed, but if the trainer didn’t have too many other expensive Pokémon they’d be able to afford it.

Question: Did infernus die at Shamouti?
Answer: Infernus didn’t die in Shamouti, but he would have died without medical attention (or the Fire Sphere shoved into him).

Question: How come Ash got his pokemon so strong in his rookie year?
Answer: Yep, Ash is the combination of the perfect circumstances, basically.

Question: On the types of energy.
Answer: Yep. For Traveler there’s only a few ‘types’ of energy. There’s generic/normal energy which is just raw energy blasts like Hyper Beam. Psychic energy, Draconic, Fairy, then Distortion is the one we see most frequently (aside from psychic).

Question: On the amount of times Groudon and Kyogre have woken up.
Answer: I won’t say much, but Groudon and Kyogre have awoken several times during humanity’s existence.

Question: Does the international police/Looker exist in Traveler? If so, who do they answer to, and how do they enforce their laws?
Answer: Will confirm the International Police are a thing in Traveler. They work in all primary regions, sort of a way to help coordinate efforts for international crime. They tend to be about as strong as a powerful Gym Trainer - tough enough to take on most opponents, but someone like Pierce would be terrifying.

Question: And your thoughts on his [Looker’s] team?
Answer: I have to really come up with anything beyond his Toxicroak, but I like what y’all are coming up with.

Question: What happens in the event of a tie in the Indigo Conference? Who gets to move on? Is it which mon falls down last or some other criteria?
Answer: Pretty much the last one to fall. A rematch would only really be possible for the Finals - dealing with a tie would be a nightmare logistically.

Question: On the strength of Mewtwo, Mew and Lugia.
Answer: Mewtwo, Mew, and Lugia are roughly even matched in the current day, but before Lugia sundered the Birds it was roughly nine times as powerful, as was Ho-Oh before jt uplifted the Beasts. Unown are roughly the same power level as well, though can be even more dangerous under certain masters. The Ghost is definitely much, much weaker than any of the above.

Question: What makes one Concept more or less powerful than another?
Answer: A big part of the Concepts and how they manifest is related to their effect on the physical world, although there are exceptions!

Question: On the size of the regions.
Answer: Also, as for the regions, I tend to view them the size of large American states, but there’s some variety. Kanto is roughly 500-700 miles wide, haven’t settled on something 100%. I try to stay away from hard numbers and generally just give estimations.

Question: Is Ho oh guardian of the sky?
Answer: Ho-Oh is the Guardian of the Sky, but it’s been more implied in Traveler than outright stated.

Question: So even though Rayquaza is tied to the sky, he's not necessarily its guardian?
Answer: Yep, not to go into too much detail but Rayquaza is Sky. The Guardians tend to be a bit more...human. They’re the glorified janitors that help maintain the balance of the full Concept.

Question: Who'd win in an arm wrestling competition? Infernus or Bruiser?
Answer: I think Bruiser is strong enough to beat Infernus if they were hypothetically able to arm wrestle. Would be a race against time before his arm burned off, but it would only take two seconds for him to win.

Question: On Suicune’s influence on humans.
Answer: Yep Suicune is kind of poisonous for anyone with ambition. Peace and serenity is good in small doses, but if you’re engulfed in it you turn flat and still, like a calm pond. No change, no impetus to grow or adapt.

Question: Do we refer to the beasts by gender or 'It'?
Answer: Yep, currently ‘it’. Like [redacted] said, they’ve risen above that. They’re more Legendary than mortal at this point.

Question: Does the calming effect [produced by Suicune] affect Raikou and Entei?
Answer: Yes, it does! The Beasts balance each other as the Birds do.

Question: Why no Entei? Wouldn’t that just increase Ash’s love for battle? And his fortitude? [see question 530]
Answer: Entei wouldn’t be the worst for Ash - I think that would be Raikou - but it’s more that Ash was already stretched to his limits. In the span of less than a week he went from training with Steven to the Rocket raid to Greenfield. I don’t think he could take any more change. He desperately needed a break and a vacation.

Question: Does Infernus know Solar Beam?
Answer: Just got back. Can confirm Infernus does know Solar Beam, although at this point it doesn’t mean much for him.

Question: On how much the general population know about the Legendary Pokemon.
Answer: Yep so much of what humans know now is mostly hearsay or extrapolation.

Question: On whether Oz can learn Teleport.
Answer: Yep, Oz can learn it. I probably will end up giving it to her just in case, although she has some other alternatives for mobility that’ll save some stamina. [see question 40] [see question 41]

Question: What were the reasonings for some of the team choices? Torrent, Seeker and Tangrowth in particular I suppose.
Answer: Sounds good! Tangela just really interested me in terms of physiology, plus it had kind of a bad reputation back in the day. I was playing Pokémon Stadium and really liked Tangela’s animations and that got me on the path to using him lol.

Question: Who actually is Ash's cornerstone?
Answer: Currently, I’d say Infernus. His evolution kinda skyrocketed him because of his body heat, though Torrent and Tangrowth aren’t long behind. [see question 421]

Question: How common is it for carry limits to be removed from non League trainers?
Answer: Not especially common. The average trainer really struggles to maintain even a full team of six - the limit is there to keep people from biting off more than they can chew.

Question: On Lance’s willingness to tease Ash about a crush.
Answer: Lance is someone I feel would poke fun occasionally at Ash’s crush, but would also do it very sparingly. He doesn’t want to make Ash too self-conscious or embarrassed about it - having a little crush is pretty much  the only normal thing about Ash, and Lance is terrified to threaten that (plus wants Ash to still feel comfortable opening up to him). There’s a reason that as soon as Lance brought it up he also mentioned his own crush on Glacia when he was younger.

Question: On the relationship between Giovanni and Surge.
Answer: Yep. Mutual respect - even genuine on Giovanni’s part. I think he’d have liked to have Surge as an Admin.

Question: Do Gym Leaders only use a certain type in gym battles? and aren't allowed to use others?
Answer: Nope, they have a focus on a certain type but they can basically use whatever they want! [see question 212]

Question: On what Professor Oak and Agatha were doing during their journeys.
Answer: Yep, he was doing independent research and keeping up with his studies, much to Alakazam’s joy.

Question: On Elf’s plan for the future.
Answer: I plan on Ash visiting every region, but most will be very brief. 1 or 2 chapters at most. We‘ll see the story continue in the vein it’s going currently through Hoenn, Sinnoh, and probably 10-15 chapters afterward at my current estimation.

Question: Will we see all the legends? [see question 542]
Answer: Probably not, honestly. Ash will encounter a lot of them but at a certain point he’s going to be much more prepared and able to avert most disasters before they can occur.

Question: Would you judge your version of legendaries to be similar to Fractals? All of them have an associated infinity, and they are definitely infinite. However they occupy a finite space. A 3D fractal has an infinite area, but the volume is still finite. The difference in the powers of legends to me just indicates a different iteration method, or a different starting volume. After all, Mew and Mewtwo both occupy a similar infinity, but Mewtwo has the benefit of a larger volume. Both have an infinite area, but one is indeed larger than the other. I suppose this makes the volume, whatever the concept itself is, such as the amount of water on the planet for Kyogre. Though if that is the case, Dialga and Palkia may have an infinite volume as the universe may be infinitely big, making them a 4D fractal in this analogy. It falls apart a little, but would you say this encompasses any of your views for legends in Traveler?
Answer: I’d say yes. To be honest, some of that goes above my head but I get the gist of it!

Question: How long ago did Sabrina conquer Saffron?
Answer: Yep, I’d say like 10 year or so ago. One of those situations where the League was like “This isn’t traditional, but if we can get an awesome psychic on our side then okay”.

Question: On how Giovanni’s deposition affected the amount of Trainers getting the Earth Badge.
Answer: See, and the funny thing is that so few people got the Earth Badge from fighting Giovanni that not having a Gym Leader there barely affects anyone.

Question: How long have the Conferences(Particularly Indigo/Silver) been around for? Has the prospect of getting the chance to challenge the Elite Four if you win always been around?
RelatedTags: ['Champion', 'Conference', 'Elite Four', 'Gym Leader', 'Indigo', 'Kanto', 'League', 'Lords of Kanto', 'Secession of 827', 'Silver Conference', 'Taimu']
Answer: Indigo and Silver Conferences have been around for a few hundred years, evolving out of regional championships designed to hunt out the strongest fighters. There have been several different methods of choosing Champions, and eventually it was decided to essentially be a tournament based on qualified individuals - back then, the people who would have been involved were generally powerful nobles or clansmen (aka people who could afford to support several pokemon). The current system evolved from that set up.

Question: On the difference between a Lord and a Gym Leader. [see question 547]
Answer: Yep, kind of a consolation prize with the stipulation that it (usually) wouldn’t pass on to their descendants.

Question: Was Cinnabar always part of the Indigo League since Taimu?
Answer: Yep, Cinnabar swore allegiance to Taimu. Acted as a very important port and one of Kanto’s links to Altomare (and thus Hoenn).

Question: How strong was Taimu’s team when he was alive? How would he compare to current champion / e4?
Answer: Taimu was a monster for his time. Mamoru was always a giant with immense physical strength and durability. Chinatsu was nowhere near as powerful, but she was still vicious, brilliant, and blessed by Fire. Shinobu was a force to be reckoned with, a physical powerhouse with a ton of speed. They were essentially unmatched in their era.

Question: Incidentally the only other battle Taimu fought against someone capable of being called an equal would probably be the Ryuujin? [see question 550]
Answer: Yep, the Ryuujin was the Cerulean Champion and son of the Cerulean Lord.

Question: Isn’t there a river near Cerulean? Nugget Bridge and the Cerulean caves and all?
Answer: Yep, there’s tons of waterways throughout Cerulean’s territory. In the games there’s the river by Nugget Bridge that opens into the sea.

Question: On how Lance would fare in Taimu’s place.
Answer: I think Lance would be phenomenal against human and Pokémon enemies, it’s just the unbeatable odds he’s facing now that are really grinding him down. He feels hopeless.

Question: On what is keeping Lance down.
Answer: Yeah, there’s definitely a good degree of guilt there! Poor Lance, just trying to be a good guy but decided to be Champion at the worst possible time.

Question: If Mewtwo didn’t do anything would the Legends have awakened in the distant future?
Answer: Yep, the Legends would have awoken over the course of decades or centuries anyways!

Question: Who made the Chosen One prophecy?
Answer: And [redacted] is correct - the prophecy was created by a thousand psychics who unmade themselves in order to create a constant in the universe: a Chosen One, with at least the potential to prevent the Birds from causing too much destruction.

Question: What was the kill count of the birds?
Answer: I mean, over their lifetime they’ve wiped out millions of living things each, easily. That’s an incredibly tiny estimation. They’re old. Pokémon are hardy enough to run or survive brief exposures, but even the Fractured Birds are enough to obliterate everything for miles upon miles around even when holding back.

Question: Post sealing kill count would be Zapdos? [see question 557]
Answer: Probably since it’s more active.

Question: Would the Beasts be the Youngest Legends excusing Mewtwo?
Answer: Yep!

Question: How did the psychics do that? To create a constant in the unpredictable universe with conceptual creatures such as the legends? [see question 556]
Answer: One way to think of it is to consider the Fragmentation of the Birds in the first place - an unnatural act, but one that could be accomplished with a sufficient amount of power sacrificed.

Question: On why Ash is so strong.
Answer: Ash is also in a unique situation where the whole team is obsessively loyal to him haha. Makes it a little easier to maintain a cohesive unit, even if cracks and rivalries are still there due to the friction between different personalities.

Question: Do you think Infernus would get along with any other fire type ash caught?
Answer: As long as the fire type could keep up haha. He’d probably get possessive though.

Question: If Mewtwo was correct in his delusions, and killed Xernias, would that kill himself too?
Answer: I would say no, but I can’t explain why just yet.

Question: Where is Metagross' mega stone located? Is it in it's canon placement (it's front right leg) or is it somewhere else?
Answer: That’s what I’d say [Steven gives it to him when Metagross needs it]. Too risky leaving it plain to see - although I can also imagine Metagross having the Mega Stone implanted within it.

Question: On entertainment in Traveler-verse.
Answer: Oh yeah of course. Tons of books, shows, movies, and games. Gaming in general is probably a little less developed than in our world despite more advanced technology, but not too far behind. Just less diversity I think.

Question: Would there also be games about the Great War? COD type games?
Answer: Sort of, although it would be a lot more complex than COD. Probably just adapt certain Pokémon species into ‘classes’ that can be used. Probably wouldn’t focus as much on the human side of it, or have humans on the field as targets for teams to wipe out in the rounds.

Question: We know that 1000 psychic’s gave up their life/bodies to create the great prophecy, but what exactly did it do? Did it ensure Ash would make it till shalmunti, or did it just allow him to be born, and if the second, what would have happened if he died earlier in his journey? Would the prophecy make sure a chosen one was always available to save the world, or was it all resting on Ash living, or was Ash’s luck influenced by the prophecy? And if that, did it ensure he was strong enough to have a chance at completion by having him get as strong as he did, or was that just circumstance?
Answer: So basically, the psychics has no effect on Ash’s journey overall. What they did when they unmade themselves was create the Concept of the Prophecy/Chosen One.

Question: Who else is in that list? [see question 567]
Answer: A bit too into spoiler territory, but I’ll offer a few:

Question: Are the cornerstones of Masters considered base Champion level?
Answer: On average, I’d say yes! It’s not an absolute rule though.

Question: On how painful evolutions are.
Answer: I will say most evolutions aren’t that painful. Uncomfortable and mildly painful more than anything, except when major changes occur. Nidoran to Nidorino wasn’t especially painful, but it was a lot worse going from Nidorino to Nidoking because of how much his body changed if that makes sense.

Question: If Alakazam can make psychic swords can it project other weapon shapes?
Answer: Yeah, psychic energy can be shaped however the psychic chooses. It does take extra effort for actual shapes - in most cases it’s most effective to shoot a beam or just grip with psychic power.

Question: Is she [Oz] 6ft like Infernus?
Answer: I’d say she’s a few inches over six feet. Electivire always struck me as having a ton of physical bulk. She’d be larger if she’d waited a bit longer, but she’ll grow into it.

Question: How did humans survive in Travelers' early world, given how delicate they are compared to pokemon?
Answer: In the earliest days, it was often a result of partnering with local wild Pokémon, facilitated by psychic abilities and Aura users. Certain psychics were curious about humanity and taught gifted humans how to access their psychic powers, offering them a valuable tool for communicating with Pokémon and defending themselves.

Question: If A water type pokemon gets Moltres’ feather then will it go through some sort of mutation?
Answer: I think the water type would really struggle with Moltres’ Feather, perhaps even being entirely incompatible and causing it to damage the Pokémon.

Question: Would you reckon it depends on the Pokemon itself or is it a general thing? [see question 574]
Answer: Majorly on the Pokémon itself - I think some water-types would have the right temperament and force of will to adapt to it instead of being slowly destroyed. Gyarados would probably do quite well with the Feather.

Question: Is this statement [an Alakazam being able to lift several hundred pounds] still accurate with regards to power levels?
Answer: Ah, yes that’s still accurate then. Several hundred pounds isn’t that much for an Alakazam, but it does take some focus and effort.

Question: So no regular animals?
Answer: Yeah, normal animals never evolved to a point where we’d recognize them as fish, reptiles, mammals, etc. Bacteria and very basic organisms evolved, but were constantly disrupted. Pokémon and humans are the only complex life to really exist.

Question: How much does the average high level trainer/coordinator make? What about the Wandering masters? And how does this money compare to other jobs?
Answer: The average high level trainer is pretty easily making 100-200k - the best can hit up into the millions through sponsorship deals, but most get snatched up by the League before that point. They’re a valuable commodity, and that’s if they aren’t popular competition battlers racking up show fees and contracts. High level ACE make a similar amount, probably hitting a ceiling of 300k. The League loves to keep its trainers happy, though, and is meritocratic enough to institute performance based bonuses.

Question: If the trainer has joined the League, does he still have the option to commit to sponsorship deals side by side?
Answer: And it depends - ACE or Rangers? Probably not. Somebody who is more of just on retainer would be fine though.

Question: How high would the tax brackets gotta be for such high incomes?
Answer: Not too high. I figure the League largely taxes industry and makes their money off skimming from trainer bets, tournaments, entertainment, etc. Plus whatever they seize from criminal organizations. Not something I’ve fleshed out too much lol.

Question: The God-King existed some time before Taimu, right?
Answer: Yep! Just as a rough breakdown there’s about five centuries between Hiro beginning the Wataru legacy and Taimu. The God-King’s legacy occurred an undisclosed amount of time beforehand.

Question: On Sinnoh after the God-King’s era.
Answer: The west [side of Sinnoh] was a bit more peaceful than the east. It had Mt. Coronet separating it from most of the warlike powers, and they primarily were either drawn into war through alliances or minor border squabbles and raids on their neighbors.

Question: On Lugia and Ho-Oh’s current strength. [see question 522]
Answer: Just caught up! Wanted to clarify that Lugia and Ho-Oh are both on an equal of level of power at this point. A major reason for Ho-Oh guiding the Beasts through apotheosis instead of just resurrecting them was to restore the balance between it and Lugia.

Question: Lugia split the birds so long ago, why would restoring the balance be such a major reason now? [see question 583]
Answer: Yeah, have to think of the timescales involved - for timeless entities, a few thousand years is just the blink of an eye. It was something to be corrected, certainly, and ate at them both as long as it wasn’t, but from their perspective Ho-Oh got right on it.

Question: What's the lore on mystery dungeons in Traveler?
Answer: I have no current plans for mystery dungeons haha.

Question: On how Cynthia directs her Pokemon during battle.
Answer: I will say that Cynthia doesn’t exactly let her team fight with no direction. She’s like Ash in that she drills them to where they can easily make their own decisions, but also uses whistles and other unseen signals to help direct them!

Question: Does Vaporeon have the ability to liquefy itself within Traveler?
Answer: I don’t have Vaporeon as able to physically turn into water. Not most of them, anyways. You could see an extremely skilled or pseudo-Legendary Vaporeon pulling that off (similar to Chinatsu with her flames) but most Vaporeon are basically just so good at camouflaging that they’re basically indistinguishable from it while submerged.

Question: On whether Acid Armor liquifies Vaporeon and Grimer/Muk though.
Answer: My POV is that Muk and Grimer are basically liquid already. Vaporeon is too...solid for me to imagine it completely decondensing. Too much structure.

Question: What's the difference between the Pokémon used in such armies from Guardians?
Answer: In general, I don’t have an over abundance of Pokémon in ancient warfare. The average soldier wouldn’t have the support of a Pokémon, or perhaps one set to cover for a small group. Just a handful with partners, since especially at that point it was both incredibly difficult to support large Pokémon and most Pokémon wouldn’t have been trained for combat like they are now. You still see partner Pokémon all over the place, just not as many specialized for combat since it was so much more dangerous without modern medicine. Pokémon are ridiculously hardy and tough, but fighting an equal is a good way to come away with a permanent injury. Like animals, they primarily postured against one another. Pokémon partners to average soldiers were mostly just there to provide logistical support more than anything.

Question: Pewter Champion had an Onix [during Taimu’s time], I take it? [see question 589]
Answer: Yep, a fairly young Onix but in a time before TMs where raw physical power was much more dangerous.

Question: Did the Rota fight happen like in the anime, then, or was it much bigger on the human to Pokémon ratio? [see question 589]
Answer: Yeah, the Rota fight was pretty similar but the human:Pokémon ratio was definitely increased!

Question: On how big the Taimu vs. Drake was considered at the time.
Answer: Yep, the scale of Taimu vs. the Drake’s armies would’ve been like at Rota. Just a massive collection of power...and one that the current Elite Four would steamroll.

Question: What would pre-Wataru Blackthorn have been like?
Answer: Blackthorn before the Wataru was kind of a terrible backwater. Extorted resources from isolated village like Hiro’s to stay alive in the harsher times of the year. It was only through the combination of harsh, rocky terrain making it difficult to attack and the unparalleled speed and firepower of Dragonite in those days that Blackwater was converted into a superpower.

Question: Did Mew know of Mewtwo's creation as soon as it happened?
Answer: Yep. It’s sensitive enough to have immediately picked up on something so similar to it.

Question: Is there any particular reason it [Mew] didn't interfere? [see question 594]
Answer: A big part of it is boredom, as well as Mew thinking in the long-term.

Question: Were other Legends woken by Mewtwo being born? Or by his exercise of his power?
Answer: Yep, basically as soon as Mewtwo started throwing his weight around he started triggering the Awakening. Kind of like some kid just set off a firecracker outside a retirement home.

Question: Does that include Heatran by any chance? [see question 596]
Answer: Yep, Heatran is stirring as well! It’s been a little more active than most, but it’s rumbling a bit now.

Question: On Haukea’s relationship with Articuno.
Answer: Ironically Articuno has a (relatively) warm relationship with Haukea - probably the closest of any Bird and their Champion...definitely closer than Tani and Zapdos at any rate.

Question: Both [Dark types and Ghost types] of them get their powers from distortion, but why is there a difference in how they work?
Answer: Yep, Dark-types and ghosts both use Distortion, just in different ways.

Question: It's often mentioned in the story that Pokémon, although willing to hurt and scare away humans, won't intentionally kill them (barring exceptions).
Answer: Yep, the world was a pretty hostile place before the rise of the Indigo League (and even up until the last two centuries or so, really). There were certainly friendly Pokémon, but it was much less common before technology and training advanced and psychics became more commonplace.

Question: Were livestock a thing?
Answer: Livestock weren’t really a thing. You’d have groups of Pokémon that might live alongside people in a partnership, but you definitely wouldn’t find Pokémon being raised to slaughter - they wouldn’t put up with that for a second, and it’s only recently humans would even have a chance of pulling it off. You’d see large groups of hunters going after some Pokémon if desperate, but it was very rare, honestly. Most Pokémon on their own could slaughter a dozen humans, and the ones that couldn’t are probably going to be in groups.

Question: Hunting probably only really started being a logistically possible thing for them once they began to befriend groups of Pokemon like Growlithe/Arcanine.
Answer: Yep, hunting would be possible but it still runs the risk of making the wild pokemon outright hostile, which makes life much more difficult.

Question: Would Pokémon hunt humans, then?
Answer: Yep, absolutely. Not all, but there are plenty of crueler species like Sneasel who’d happily hunt a human, if only for the sport.

Question: On the appearance of Jon and Amelia.
Answer: So for Jon, I always imagined him as a short, stocky kid. Not chubby but kind of burly and bulky. Plain brown hair, and that’s about as much as I’ve thought of his actual appearance haha. His personality is most what I think about.

Question: On the difference between Bruno’s early and current chapter’s strength.
Answer: But yeah, Bruno has bad luck to be the first Elite Four member to appear and one when I hadn’t 100% settled into the power levels. Glad I built at least some excuses in for Ash’s luck there.

Question: Can anyone think of a named character in Traveler who has a Ponyta or Rapidash?
Answer: I’m pretty sure Neesha will have a Rapidash - I tried to include the whole team.

Question: On the advantages of Ash working for the League.
Answer: One perk of working so closely with the League is that Ash has specially trained support mons to save him (and me) the trouble of managing an additional teammate.

Question: would it be possible for Dazed to use a liquid as a foci if it was viscous enough? Are foci best when homogenous?
Answer: I don’t think the foci actually do anything themselves. I think of them purely as a mental tool to help psychics focus and retain clarity of thought - something to center themselves around, I suppose. Theoretically, anything could be used as a foci. I think metal, crystal, and stone tend to be favored because they are difficult to damage, and the shine of metal and crystal catches the eye...plus, some psychics might just think it looks pretty. I imagine they get a massive personal attachment to their foci.

Question: Do we know anything about farming in the Traveler verse?
Answer: What was the farming question again?

Question: On the existence of normal animals.
Answer: Yep, anything too complex would just be instantly outcompeted. You have bacteria, plants, fungi, protists, etc but normal animals just can’t survive in a world filled with Pokémon for the most part. I imagine some small insects exist, but it’s not something I’ll address in story.

Question: On Galar.
Answer: I see Galar as being more restrictive. They’re pretty isolationist, and as a result it’s easy to be a big fish in a small pond.

Question: The origin of the cape of the drake of the Wataru clan?
Answer: Yep, basically it’s just a status symbol... plus it looks cool and very dramatic when you’re flying on a Dragonite.

Question: Does a Zoroark’s illusions show up on a digital camera, or does it just fool organic lifeforms?
Answer: I can’t speak for canon, but in Traveler at least I have them as actual physical illusions while a psychic’s illusions are limited to the mind.

Question: Are there any rules in the league that regulate people using family members or other people's pokemon to just sweep through gym challenges or low level tournaments?
Answer: Yep. It’s handled pretty much on a case by case basis, but generally transferring a Pokémon over to a family member or friend (or anyone really) comes with some heavy stipulations on entering competitions. It’s pretty rare for the most part because most Pokémon wouldn’t agree with it, although there are exceptions.

Question: On Ash’s future durability.
Answer: Ash will definitely become more adept over time, but I want him to always stay a squishy human haha.

Question: On where Cynthia was raised.
Answer: Plus she grew up in Celestic Town, which is full of all kinds of lore.

Question: Who was the one pushing for the S.S Anne?
Answer: I can imagine Giovanni supporting it, but it overall being a bit of a pet project for the League - more of a situation he took advantage of than one he engineered.

Question: Do ghosts need sleep? Or is it only pokemon with insomnia that don't?
Answer: I imagine ghosts don’t need actual sleep for the most part, although they do require rest to heal.

Question: Are nightmares technically infused with distortion within Traveler due to their connection with Darkrai? If so, is this why nightmares tend to cause problems for most Hypno, much like how Dusknoir prevented her using her powers for so long?
Answer: I love that and that’s a brilliant thought I never considered! Unfortunately I have to say no, if only because I have the nightmares causing stress in Hypno mostly because of a psychic phenomena where they encounter the same feelings of stress, anxiety, and fear from nightmares as the source of the nightmare would. I currently have Darkrai’s influence over nightmares more of a side effect of its nature than as its Concept of that makes sense.

Question: So for Xerneas and Yveltal, I'd assume their concepts are Life and Death, but how would that work in something like Traveler.
Answer: So I have Xerneas and Yveltal as having aspects representing Life and Death, but at their core they’re a little bigger. Life and Death, Beginning and End, the Alpha and the Omega…

Question: Does Charizard get both its mega evolutions, or just one of them?
Answer: Yep, Charizard gets both. It just relies on whichever Stone is present.

Question: On Metagross’ psychic sense.
Answer: One difficulty might come in that Metagross is able to sense minds with the same psychic sense as Dazed. It’s one of the ways it managed to snipe Plume so perfectly.

Question: Could Metagross plausibly maintain shields, vacuum, and magnet rise all at once, though?
Answer: It likely could for a short duration, but Metagross is more likely to assess the situation and stop maintaining whatever effect is least useful to conserve energy.

Question: On a Pseudo Legendary natural strength.
Answer: I will say that our view is being skewed by seeing the absolute cream of the crop. Gym Leaders, Masters, Champions, etc. Average pseudos are naturally strong and predisposed to extreme power, but most trained Pokémon can easily close the gap. Techniques and strategy make them less unassailable than they were in the past.

Question: How does Metagross handle Spiritomb?
Answer: Not very well - an average Metagross has plenty of options even without its psychic abilities, but it’s kind of like a Metagross having to fight blindfolded, with a migraine, and a hand tied behind its back.

Question: Do we know all of the eight ancient kanto territory symbols?
Answer: Yep, I basically just used the different badges as the different symbols! There are Pokémon heavily associated with different cities such as Gyarados or Gengar, but the symbols etched into Mamoru’s armor are just the badge symbols. I picture them as mostly being carved into his chest, although they probably run down to his upper belly!

Question: Do they run vertically or horizontally or is there no order? [see question 626]
Answer: Vertically. Well, I imagine there are two columns of four each laid next to each other that run down his armor.

Question: Were they ever described in an order or something? [see question 626]
RelatedTags: ['Celadon City', 'Cerulean City', 'Cinnabar Island', 'Fuchsia City', 'Kanto', 'Lavender Town', 'Mamoru', 'Pewter City', 'Saffron City', 'Taimu', 'Vermillion City', 'Viridian City']
Answer: I never described the order, although I do have a rough estimate of his [Taimu’s] journey in my head.

Question: Do the regions in Traveler have anything akin to WMDs or Nuclear Weapons?
Answer: Sort of! Like [redacted] said, the Pokémon world is capable of manufacturing powerful weapons able to affect pokemon. They’re primarily used for sieges - pyrobombs, cryobombs, and similar technology. Loaded up into battleships and things like that.

Question: Any [weapon technologies] that exists mostly are within the realm of supporting their Pokemon and keeping the trainer themselves safe right?
Answer: Yep. There’s plenty of that. Most human weapons technology is built around supporting their Pokémon or buying the human some time to run away. Pokémon are just too tough and powerful to fight on even footing.

Question: How old is Nidoking or Torrent or Dazed?
Answer: Nidoking was roughly a year old when Ash first caught him. Torrent is about the same, probably a few months older. I imagine Dazed to be anywhere from 2-4. I know it’s not extremely certain dates, but I tend to think of things in a range instead of specifics.

Question: When would each of Ash's team reach their full maturity?
Answer: I’ll throw some estimates out! It is admittedly tied heavily to how old they are when they reach their final evolutionary stage, and Ash’s team are kind of ridiculously early on that front.

Question: I imagine Sneasel and Weavile focus more on developing as quickly as possible in the wild since they live pretty fast and loose lifestyles?
Answer: Yep, they can continue advancing in elemental and Distortional abilities, but Weavile develop quickly once they evolve.

Question: Is there any hair or fur you imagine Bruiser having?
Answer: Nope, I imagine the Machoke line to have leathery or reptilian skin.

Question: How do Tangrowth reproduce?
Answer: Honestly, I can see Tangrowth reproducing fairly normally. Eggs and all that, though I can see the species as having some form of asexual reproduction.

Question: Were Legends the only beings we can consider Gods?
Answer: So there are a lot of layers of worship. It’s very regional and dependent on location.

Question: considering that the 1000 psychics sacrificing themselves made a constant. Could material sacrifices of significant value to the person doing it, have an effect on reality?
Answer: Mm, I’d say no to that. A big part of the Prophecy being shaped is that the thousand psychics went into it willingly and had the skill and knowledge to accomplish their goal.

Question: Is Aron the oldest team member?
Answer: Nope, I’d say that’s probably Oz. She’s got a good few years on the rest of the team.

Question: On the future growth of Ash’s team.
Answer: They’re going to age at fine wine. I’d say most of Ash’s team is at 80-85% of what they would be if they were fully mature. Still incredibly powerful, but their skills and experience far outstrip their bodies. [see question 351]

Question: How/why Sinnoh’s God King crossed the Dark World to conquer Kanto/Johto?
Answer: Not to go into too much detail, but the God-King’s unstoppable advance was stopped just north of Rota, which itself is right north of Indigo. Basically at the end of the Ore Mountains.

Question: Were the barriers of Mew and the Tree of Life always there [in Rota]?
Answer: Tree of Beginning was always there and that’s all I’ll say for now!

Question: Do moves like u turn and baton pass work like they do in game?
Answer: Eh, not really. I treat the switching out more as game mechanics, or something that can be used effectively outside of Conference matches. Useful in a real battle - I see U Turn as something that basically leaves an opening on an opponent for the switch out to take advantage of.

Question: Did Oak ever get the chance to talk with Chinatsu?
Answer: Yep. That’s one of those things where I imagine a new Champion is rushed to meet Chinatsu basically as soon as the match is over. The official ceremony where Oak handed back the cape came shortly after...although admittedly I’m fuzzy on some of the details in-story that I’ve mentioned.

Question: How close is the colony to Meteor Falls?
Answer: Not too close! It’s to the northeast of Rustboro, but probably 50-60 miles off Meteor Falls.

Question: How strong is the average ACE squad member and how strong is the average commander?
Answer: Average ACE member is about equivalent to Top 16 in Conference. Think Jessica. Commanders tend to be around Top 4 to Finalist level, occasionally pushing into Master territory.

Question: Do Agatha's Pokémon have names?
Answer: She did not! Due to her abilities they could communicate on a pretty deep level, so verbal names weren’t necessary for her. It was kind of like what we see with Pokémon perspectives where she’s mostly referring to them mentally by what they mean to her, or a string of words meant to embody their place.

Question: Is the Traveler Earth/World smaller than our own?
Answer: Nope, it’s at least the same size. One thing to remember is that humans only inhabit a tiny fraction of the world. The vast majority is still solely the territory of wild Pokémon.

Question: But don’t they at least have satellites?
Answer: Yep, it’s very recent. Too many things that can mess with them - one very large, green serpent in particular isn’t a fan. It’s not immediate, but it makes them expensive

Question: Is pollution a large problem in Traveler, I understand that Muk and other Pokemon exist but would it cause global warming?
Answer: Humans haven’t caused any real changes such as global warming due to their smaller population and more efficient technology. There were some growing pains with the industrial revolution, but the hostile reactions of Pokémon to pollution forced a relatively quick transition to more efficient and less polluting sources of energy.

Question: Do psychics and ghosts poop?
Answer: I can’t believe I’m answering this, but yes. Although Dazed subsists on dreams, it’s essentially used as a source of energy similar to how plants use light to drive the reactions of photosynthesis or certain bacteria are able to produce energy using chemicals. So it’s just a source of energy, but it’s used to drive chemical processes in her body. Which technically makes her an animal autotroph which is whole interesting thing on its own.

Question: So does this reaction produce no waste? Like, I don't specifically mean feces, it might be interesting if she emits vapor or something as a result of the reactions. [see question 650]
Answer: No, she [Dazed] does still produce waste. Still gotta get waste products out of her body, and given her otherwise mammalian physiology a Hypno would already have systems built in for that instead of developing an entirely different strategy of waste removal.

Question: How did Champion Taimu and Drake Ryoto look?
Answer: Taimu has black hair and bright blue eyes, and that’s about as much description as I’ve given haha. I figure he generally wore indigo colored robes, although the white Champion’s mantle hadn’t been incorporated at that point. Relatively average height, although he was taller than most peasants due to a better diet.

Question: Did it [Galar] leave the National League too?
Answer: The National League is made up of Indigo, Hoenn, and Sinnoh. Unova, Kalos, and Galar have their own individual Leagues. Everyone’s on good terms at this point, but the other regions wanted to maintain independence.

Question: What's that archipelago south of Kalos?
Answer: Onulo is an invention of mine! Haven’t worked it into Traveler or Recollections at this point.

Question: It was already mentioned that steel was a rare commodity in the past of Traveler's universe, generally only obtained through extremely rare Steelix corpses or fallen meteors. However, was that just for steel specifically? Was crude iron used in common cases, or even copper/bronze? Or were mineral veins guarded by wild rock/steel types and thus similarly inaccessible?
Answer: Generally high-quality iron was difficult to find primarily in Indigo. Kind of a reference to Japan’s relatively poor mineral resources. They had access to resources, but had to be creative when it came to metals. Lots of scavenging from Magnemite, Steelix, etc and also making deals with local Pokémon to help them.

Question: If the Fairy type can't sustain itself outside those three regions, then what happens to the Eevee who somehow become Sylveon outside them? Is it just impossible to become Sylveon at all or are they just instantly boned?
Answer: I don’t want to reveal much at this point, but I will say it’s impossible to become Sylveon outside those regions.

Question: Is Spiritomb mortal? Age-wise at least.
Answer: Nope, Spiritomb cannot die naturally. They’re unique in that they’re physically bound to the Keystone. Most ghosts have their corporeal form constantly degrading, thus requiring Aura to be ingested in order to maintain it. Keystones are immune to that degradation, since Spiritomb are designed for longevity. Don’t want your unique super weapon dying!

Question: And have we seen all bigger settlements (at least as big as Pallet town) through Ash’s eyes or are there more like in the Anime, but they weren’t relevant to the story?
Answer: Oh no, there are tons and tons of smaller settlements spread out across the regions. Most just aren’t important for me to go over haha. There are hundreds of “Dandra Towns” basically. Small, but still important areas.

Question: On who is the strongest Gym Leader.
Answer: IMO opinion, strongest Gym Leader is Volkner. In Traveler, at least. I have him as a genuine Master, where Clair isn’t at that level and Sabrina doesn’t care to hit that level.

Question: Which region has the strongest elite four on average in your opinion?
Answer: Going back to the earlier question, I’d say that Sinnoh currently has the strongest overall Elite Four.

Question: Aaron is expected to be Champion, how old is he?
Answer: He’s a few years older than Ash. Still ridiculously young for a Master, but Ash has basically broken every record lol.

Question: What records did Lance set, apart from Champion at 16? Master at 13?
Answer: Master at...14? Been a while since I reread his portions. But yep, Champion at 16. Didn’t really fully step into the role for a few years, but he technically had the title and was being trained for it immediately.

Question: Isn’t Leon ash’s age atm?
Answer: Leon is just a few years older.

Question: On the battle between Karen and Ash.
Answer: Also, I will say Karen wasn’t really holding back. Could she have been more brutal and vicious? Yes, but so could Ash. But she was also using it as a valuable teaching opportunity for Ash. The Elite Four are all about pushing themselves to new heights, and that requires going as hard as possible (without causing permanent damage).

Question: Do we know what mega Lance has?
Answer: Safe to say Elite Four members and Champions have every possible Mega Evolution for their team! They just have to choose carefully since Mega Evolution is a strain on their body.

Question: Someone that I can truly call a prodigy is Caitlin, is she in Traveler?
Answer: Yep, Caitlin does exist in Traveler.

Question: On Ash’s birthday.
Answer: His birthday is early May, can’t remember the exact date. I have the Indigo Conference in March, so Ash began at the beginning of the new season in April. He turned 11 in Chapter 3, 12 in Chapter 32.

Question: Do you know the difference between for example electric legendaries? Not in terms of strength but just like their being.
Answer: Primarily different Concepts. I have them all in a list, but for just a few: Zapdos is Lightning/Fall/Spring, Thundurus is more of Thunder/Storm. As for Zekrom, I have it as Ideals - kind of the laser focus of someone focusing on upholding their ideals if that makes any sense.

Question: Do you have a particular explanation as to how Decidueye turns into a ghost type upon evolving from Dartrix?
Answer: Nothing too fancy! Just figure that the evolution naturally gives it more of an affinity for manipulating Distortional energy.

Question: How do moves that are taught by Move Tutors in the game work in Traveler? Are they rare enough moves that aren't widespread enough to be made into TMs, so you have to find tutors specialized in teaching them?
Answer: Moves taught by tutor are usually just fairly rare, or else require intensive, specialized training to use effectively. Or are secrets held by Type Masters such as Blast Burn.

Question: How much focus/power would Electric Terrain require to maintain?
Answer: I imagine a massive upfront cost, then actual maintenance wouldn’t be too bad! Just a bit of focus and energy.

Question: What was the situation in Indigo around Taimu's time? Were there many big nations? Were there very notable ones that had great influence (discounting the Wataru)? Were all the Lords (now Gym Leaders) underneath Taimu and Ryoto Guardians/Leaders of their own tribe/nation, or were there some who simply were notable warriors of great renown?
Answer: In general, Indigo was divided up into city-states. Constantly shifting alliances, and lots of city-states would ally against a greater threat for one war and then prepare to go to war with their former ally.

Question: How does the happiness evolution thing work in Traveler?
Answer: Happiness is basically normally an evolution that comes from a feeling of contentment and being accepted by a group in social Pokémon - so once a Pokémon matures and has reached a place of esteem in the social hierarchy that’s usually when it will evolve. [see question 118]

Question: Since Tangrowth grows forever, does that mean he is biologically immortal, will continually grow more powerful, or he just will always be able to regenerate lost limbs?
Answer: Tangrowth is essentially biologically immortal and will live pretty much up until something kills him.

Question: What about weakened 1/3 Moltres (like when he fought Lance) vs evolved Mamoru?
Answer: It’s one of those things where as stupidly powerful as demigods like Mamoru, the King, Chinatsu, and the others are compared to normal Pokémon, they’re still nothing compared to a Legend. The power gap between them is painful.

Question: On the Unown.
Answer: So, I will say that Unown can’t be destroyed. You can condense or store them (like the Unown tablet) but they’re constants.

Question: How old was Champion Sam when he became champion?
Answer: Oak was roughly 20 when he became Champion.

Question: What happens if you attempt to teleport a dark type attack?
Answer: A psychic would pretty much be completely unable to affect or teleport a Distortion based attack.

Question: So does Orre use primarily Double Battles like the games, or is it Singles like everywhere else?
Answer: Honestly, Orre is just based around using whatever you have available. Their colosseums are generally double - that’s the max most trainers can easily command at once.

Question: Has Elf or one of the mods specifically ever clarified whether current Ash would have won the conference before?
Answer: If current Ash faced Conference Michael, he would win if the lineups remained the same. But if Michael was able to adjust and was aware of Ash’s tactics, he still might be able to pull off a victory.

Question: Where would he [Ash] get the win from? [see question 680]
Answer: Just general improvement honestly. Ash hasn’t made gigantic leaps like in Kanto but he’s had very good, solid, steady progression. Becoming more efficient, tightening up techniques, and in general just becoming more skilled and thoughtful.

Question: On what are the factors to be considered when teleporting.
Answer: Yep, volume and mass both are factors, and Metagross has plenty of both. That’s also one of the reasons conventional trade routes only recently died out - it wasn’t feasible to transport anything but small, lightweight cargo across long distances until pokeball technology was developed.

Question: On Ash’s willingness to follow a meal plan.
Answer: To be fair, if there is any kid ever who’s willingly setting up a meal plan and following it rigorously, it’s Ash.

Question: Does that mean Surge is on his way to being a true Master Level? Or do you think his team has sorta plateaued?
Answer: Yeah, Surge loves his team and being strong. He has the potential to become an Electric Master if he set his mind to it, but that’s honestly just not his priority. He’s too invested in his people and role as a Gym Leader to dedicate himself to that path.

Question: On Neesha vs. Jessica.
Answer: I would say Neesha absolutely beats Jessica.

Question: Are there dual type specialists?
Answer: 100%. Probably not something people necessarily identify themselves as, but there’s a lot of types with tons of overlap. Just look at Lorelei.

Question: How long ago was the last war?
Answer: Roughly 30 years ago!

Question: Are there any League Pokémon squads that function without trainers? Aside from the Teleportation division.
Answer: Yep, there are squads that function without trainers, or at least only check in with League operatives every so often. Lots of scouts that monitor the wilderness and wild Pokémon, Castform deployed to fix all the weather issues, and other functional uses.

Question: How does Victini work as a concept?
Answer: In Traveler, Victini is less the Concept of Victory and more the Concept of Probability.

Question: Do trainers need to retake badges after each conference tho?
Answer: Trainers do need to go through the Kanto badges again if they want to requalify for the Indigo Conference.

Question: What other minor gyms are there?
Answer: Minor Gyms are set up by the Leagues. There’s about 3 per each territory, generally staffed by veteran Gym Trainers for that territory who are looking to gain experience and eventually ascend to a higher role. Their purpose is generally to give the League ability to easily project force into less populated areas and protect towns away from the major cities. They basically act as law enforcement, manage wild Pokémon, support Ranger teams, etc.

Question: On Erika’s capabilities and deficiencies.
Answer: I will say Erika would be capable of getting a city running. Is it her forte? Not really. But she’d be fantastic at the logistical side and organizing things. She’d be good at picking the people needed to do the groundwork while making things run relatively smoothly in the meantime

Question: How important is privacy for the general population, or the League, taking psychics into account? Like we know they are used for a vetting process of League members, but how much is the Pokemon allowed to probe, everything? Could a psychic be used during a census to preemptively attack criminals? Would the people be ok with that and accept it like we do when going through a metal detector?
RelatedTags: ['Crime', 'Dark type', 'Ghost type', 'Gym Leader', 'League', 'Lords of Kanto', 'Psychic type', 'Technology']
Answer: Honestly, psychic scans are pretty standard fare at this point. People are largely accustomed to it - the wilds and cities are patrolled by psychics that maintain a surface level scan. Not enough to really read thoughts, but enough to pick up on issues. The metal detector analogy you have is a good one. It’s one of those things where in our world a psychic scan would be horribly intrusive - in the Pokémon world they’re used to it.

Question: Would gym leaders now have to go through annual scans?
Answer: Yeah there’ll be frequent scans, probably once every few months in the Indigo League.

Question: His [Giovanni’s] mother was the former boss wasn’t she?
Answer: Yep, that applies to Traveler!

Question: Would that be headed by the E4 or someone else in the league? [see question 694]
Answer: Most likely an Elite Four member (who has also been vetted by several different psychics).

Question: On whether electricity generated by Electric type Pokemon is actual electricity.
Answer: For the electric-types, Pokémon are generating real electricity just not real lightning. They’re all capable of producing an electric charge.

Question: Are there any defining features to pierce's umbreon?
Answer: No real details that I can remind - just savage, snarling, and distinctly feral looking. Probably lots of scars, poor self-hygiene (matted fur, bloody muzzle if it’s been in a fight).

Question: Are there any distinct features to Princess? like scars?
Answer: No major scars on Princess, but lots of small ones. I see her as the type to demand perfect healing.

Question: The Pokemon powerchart, the one with Ash's pokemon and the rest, is it subject to change as the team grows, or what the Elf has planned for them to stand at at their prime?
Answer: It’s showing where they currently stand, so it is subject to change!

Question: On the average strength of a Conference participant.
