Answer: Yep, the majority of Conference battlers will be veterans. Not many rookies are able to join, let alone make it past the first few rounds. Placing in the Conference is a good way to get attention, and that’s when lucrative opportunities from the League and private organizations start rolling in.

Question: How replicable is a Metagross' strength? Say for example that Steven's Metagross goes to the colony, communicates to them all the things it does under Steven's command, what prevents the colony from "assembling" a Metagross with very close capabilities?
Answer: They do share experience, knowledge, etc. very fluidly! Going forward, the Stone’s colony will be one of, if not the, most powerful colonies in the world. They’ll be able to utilize that knowledge and Steven’s Metagross’ experience and skills to train exceptional Metagross in defense of the colony.

Question: All of his [Blaine’s] cloning innovations were after the war, right?
Answer: Yep, the cloning innovations came after the war!

Question: So were the cloning things a drunken experiment?
Answer: More of an attempt at escape.

Question: What would you title the specific arcs in Traveler?
Answer: Arc 1: Beginnings.Arc 2: Consequences.Arc 3: Rising.Arc 4: Erosion.Arc 5: Recovery.Arc 6: Immersion.Arc 7: Quandaries.

Question: Bit disappointed that [during chapter 52] there wasn't some delving into whatever business that happened with Pike though.
Answer: I remembered Pike/Drake haha. It’s just a minor footnote for Ash, though. And I think it would’ve been an awkward dinner conversation.

Question: Did you get her [Hata’s] name from the weapon?
Answer: I’ve given some of the Wataru a bit of a naming convention with that. Glaive, Hasta, Pike.

Question: I can see Lance in that naming convention but where did Clair come from? [see question 708]
Answer: I’m just having it arranged by different families - not everyone does it, and I had Clair’s mom named something different if I remember right. I wanted to show that her family didn’t have that particular tradition.

Question: When Ash goes to see Lt. Surge, would Brock be there for his training still or is Brock back in his city?
Answer: Brock will be back in Pewter - he’s completed his Gym Leader training.

Question: On whether Clair didn’t battle Gary due to lack of Pokemon available.
Answer: Admittedly, Clair has several additional Pokémon that would’ve easily filled the gaps in on her team. Gym Leaders have to have enough to go toe to toe with multiple challengers. Her vacation is a training trip, but she mostly did it just to mess with Gary.

Question: On the effect of Moltres’ feather.
Answer: Won’t touch on the others since I like this discussion, but Fire makes him [Ash] more passionate. It enflames his emotions. Amongst other things.

Question: How come Daisy has permission for carrying a full team?
Answer: After some time as a trainer it’s possible to receive a certification allowing for more. It’s not common - most people simply can’t manage a larger team - but for those with the talent and trust of a League it’s an option.

Question: On Tani.
Answer: Oh! Another thing I wanted to mention but forgot - Tani received her Feather from a Third of Zapdos, the roamer (also the one that Ash met at the power plant). Recollections Chapter 21 took place after they were sundered by Lugia, around the times the first true kingdoms and states had been properly established.

Question: So did Zapdos purposefully leave its feather behind [for Tani]?
Answer: Sort of. Zapdos scatters Feathers pretty frequently without care, but they almost always fizzle out before anything finds them (and most things that find them steer clear).

Question: What is the political structure of Rota?
Answer: It’s one of the last kingdoms still extant in the modern age and is ruled by its queen. It’s a rather small nation but one with a long, long history. Rota was originally founded by worshippers of the Tree of Beginning, and its worship and symbolism remains a prominent staple of Rotan culture. It remained independent from the Leagues, although it has close ties to Indigo and acts as a sort of territory for it in practice - they abide by League laws, support League members in the area, and in turn receive many benefits while retaining their de facto independence.

Question: Would Infernus ever learn Confuse Ray?
Answer: Yep, Infernus can use Confuse Ray although it’s not a ‘proper’ version like you’d see from a skilled dark-type or ghost like Gary’s Umbreon. That level of Confuse Ray - essentially the OHKO, crippling version - is extremely demanding.

Question: On the difference between veteran Master level Trainers and newcomers.
Answer: Yep, there’s definitely a huge difference between Masters like Agatha or Bruno and younger Masters  like Karen or Will.

Question: Plume can learn ominous wind apparently, think that'd help against confuse ray and perish song?
Answer: It could by disrupting the Distortional energies, although her best option is definitely still to fly away!

Question: How would you describe perish song, is it like an area of effect with a limited range or is it more of, if you can hear it, it'll affect you?
Answer: Yep a bit of both factors come into play! It exponentially weakens the farther from it you are, but it has a real risk to it no matter what.

Question: On the difference between Dialga and Celebi’s concept.
Answer: Yep, that’s a good way of looking at it. Dialga is more of the primal force. Celebi is an embodiment of a will/intelligence behind that force. You can kind of look at it as a similar situation to Moltres and Lance or Chinatsu.

Question: He’s still 11 right? cause I thought I saw somewhere that said he was 12.
Answer: Nope, he’s twelve now [chapter 53]. He turned 11 around Vermillion in Chapter 3 and 12 shortly before he left for Hoenn.

Question: What do you think about Torrent using ice to coat his body for defence and Sneasel slipping into the distortion realm to dodge attacks?
Answer: We’ll actually see Torrent doing something similar to that but with water instead. I think water will provide a better cushion and resistance to physical and elemental attacks than ice!

Question: On Skill Swap.
Answer: Skill Swap works in some fashion, but Inusually see it as a mental technique copying mental traits of the user onto the receiver. It’s one of those nightmare moves to try to fully incorporate!

Question: So how often has gabite destroyed an important document?
Answer: Very rarely, unfortunately. Lance is pretty responsible and learned quickly.

Question: On Plume’s strength.
Answer: The thing with Plume is that she’s absolutely terrifying in a lot of ways. In the field she’s easily one of the most valuable members of the team. Regulated matches just don’t play to her strengths in the least - confined spaces, limited area to fly and accelerate, etc. In the field, though, she’s essentially instant death. Too fast to see or hear coming, capable of sniping with Hyper Beams, and capable of disrupting enemy formations with techniques like Hurricane. She’d actually fare really, really well against most members of the team in a one-on-one. She just has terrible match ups for the most part. It’s something I hope to fix going forward.

Question: How would that affect their [Nidoking and Nidoqueen’s] evolution and sterilisation?
Answer: [Redacted] is right, as of this moment I have Nidoking and Nidoqueen as sterile for the reasons he mentioned [and it prevents bloodlines from taking over - averting the issue of hereditary monarchy. By eliminating the ability for matriarchs and patriarchs to reproduce it enforces a system where the best of the herd is elevated rather than just the children of the matriarchs and patriarchs]. Keep in mind that Nidoking would NOT naturally evolve at such a young age. In the wild he’d have had one or two decades easily to spread his genes before making the evolution to Nidoking.

Question: On why Dialga and Palkia’s name appear as a tetragram.
Answer: That said, that’s just the real world I got inspiration from. There’s plenty of in-universe reasons for culturally avoiding the names. They’re incredibly sacred and given enormous gravitas.

Question: On how Legends are regarded in Sinnoh.
Answer: Yep, I figured that Sinnoh is much more favorable to the Legends. Kanto holds great respect and reverence for them, but Sinnoh has several Legends with Concepts favorable to humanity, particularly those that offer peace and vitality instead of rampant destruction.

Question: How cooperative are the cults [Sinnoh’s] with each other?
Answer: They’re fairly distinct, but do interact on occasion. They’ve come together in the past to achieve great things, such as supporting Calanthia and ending the Weeping Era.

Question: How will Ash's sanity survive after Darkrai, Palkia and Dialga?
Answer: One advantage to Ash ‘starting small’ with the Legends is that he’s slowly being hardened by the experience. He would never have been able to withstand the Spheres at the beginning of his journey, for example.

Question: On Ash’s future growth.
Answer: Yep, I won’t go into detail but Ash has a lot of potential power at his fingertips once he becomes more aware of things.

Question: On the consequences of Ash tapping into the Concepts’ power.
Answer: Keep in mind that even if Ash receives a Brand and can theoretically tap into a Concept, it’s not safe in the least. Very much a double-edged sword and there are things that man just isn’t meant to mess with.

Question: Defenders, Learned, and Stalwart, huh?
Answer: Yep! Well, admittedly the Defenders are a military organization of Jubilife that only take inspiration from the Lake. The true order that follows Mesprit is the Empathetic.

Question: On Grey’s Scolipede.
Answer: Yep, Scolipede is basically an assassin. It’s generally used to pick apart teams and strike vulnerable members while Hydreigon takes the attention off it. Psychics don’t last long with Scolipede round.

Question: On the people who are aware of Mega Evolution.
Answer: Yep, they existed in some form in Hoenn but it was a well kept secret by the Draconids. It’s much more common in Kalos and incorporated into many of their traditions. Many Mega Stones are prized possessions of the old aristocratic families.

Question: Is any part of Infernus made of magma?
Answer: Nope, but his body is similar and includes mineral components that are superheated you essentially be magma. I imagine he’s more silicon-based in general, with minor insulated organic components. A very alien form of life from our perspective.

Question: How old are the Champions in the national league i.e Steven Cynthia and Lance?
Answer: Lance is about 27, Steven started training when Lance did but is a little under a year younger, and Cynthia just a little younger than Steven. [see question 21]

Question: On who trained Steven.
Answer: Yep, Fino Moore!

Question: Is it spoilery to ask for info on Flint?
Answer: Nah! I don’t have too much on him, honestly. Just that his Magmortar is an absolute beast. It would solidly beat Infernus if that tells you anything! [see question 436] [see question 660]

Question: On the Hashimoto.
RelatedTags: ['Agatha', 'Confuse Ray', 'Curse', 'Dark type', 'Distortion', 'Hashimoto', 'Lavender Town', 'Lords of Kanto', 'Psychic type', 'Saffron City', 'Shadow Ball']
Answer: In the past, the Hashimoto were quite plentiful. Several dozen, generally. They perfected the “stuff a screaming newborn ghost into a screaming newborn human” technique a few centuries ago and had time to refine it. When the family was butchered in the war that began the end of the reign of Lords in Indigo, only a single family unit remained. Their potent knowledge of Distortion and hybrids made them prime candidates for buying Lavender time - the Hashimoto were major parts of the defense in Saffron and the Lavender territory.

Question: So is Mega [Metagross] stronger than the ancients, like Mamoru and the nidos and Clefable and the King?
RelatedTags: ['Chinatsu', 'Clefable', 'Ghost of Lavender', 'King Under the Mountain', 'Mamoru', 'Mega Evolution', 'Teleport']
Answer: Ehhhhh, I’d say it depends. Chinatsu makes power set would obliterate Metagross. Mamoru would have a rough time against Mega Metagross, and without evolving would be beaten.

Question: What is the age difference between Karen and Lance?
Answer: Karen is 20-21. [see question 21] [see question 737]

Question: How Elf will differentiate Sidney and Karen's fighting styles, if at all?
Answer: Yep. Sidney’s approach to combat is to allow no quarter. A mix of extraordinarily intricate mental attacks and illusions, offensive power, and environmental manipulation. He’s ferocious.

Question: Can Dazed do what Sydney’s Zoroark can do if she trains at it? Maybe not the exact same thing, but similar techniques/tactics? [see question 743]
Answer: Sort of! Not actual physical manipulations, but she can get into heads and alter their perceptions.

Question: So the physical manipulations are made possible by Distortion Energy? What if she used... shaped barriers or something. Made them look different via... Light Manipulation? (Hypno do learn Flash) Bit of a stretch, but... maybe?
Answer: Yep, it would theoretically be possible to do physical illusions for Dazed but it would be very complex. In a one-on-one match a mental effect would be more straightforward.

Question: How does Lance fair against Agatha and ghosts?
Answer: As for Lance, one thing to remember is that his dragons are very, very fast for the most part. It’s hard to hit those flying targets even for Agatha’s team, and the elemental attacks and AoE are extremely well-suited for taking down her ghosts and nullifying their advantages.

Question: If Spiritomb changes from its corruption what would it look like in battle?
Answer: Spiritomb will always be able to fight in some capacity, and Cynthia would be in the perfect spot to exploit its changing abilities. Even if it reached the point where it can’t directly hurt Pokémon, sharing its positive emotions and manifesting them could end a fight just as well.

Question: When Ash gets his Ghost Pokemon and has a lifetime of devouring Ash aura/Concepts, would Ash Ghost Pokemon be different from other Ghost Types? Would Ash Ghost Pokemon need or refuse to eat other people's Auras, because of Ash’s Aura is so powerful or potent or perhaps....tasty?
Answer: Ghosts can be quite nebulous. I don’t think it would be especially different from other ghosts, but I do think it would be subtly shaped by its experiences with Ash.

Question: On Infernus vs. Champion level Pokemon.
Answer: Explanation for Infernus - he tends be as much of a raw threat as a Champion-level pokemon due to teleportation and his body heat crippling the average matchup. Overall he’s Master-level, as a Champion-leave opponent will usually be able to handily beat him, particularly if they’re prepared for him.

Question: On whether Ash took his Moon Stone and Dragon Scale with him on his journey.
Answer: Yeah there’s some inconsistency there. I’d intended on making a mention in early Hoenn of Ash leaving them in Pallet instead of taking them, but I never did.

Question: On who knows about the link between Ash and Mewtwo.
Answer: Yep, Will knows what’s up and Lance at least knows about the link. I don’t think Lance realized it was so accessible, though.

Question: How much time passed this chapter [chapter 56]?
Answer: Roughly 3 weeks. We’ll be seeing the rate of time passing pick up a fair bit from here on out, which will be great for Ash’s growth.

Question: On when the Ever Grande Conference took place.
Answer: Just to make a quick correction, the Ever Grande League was taking place when Ash first went to Hoenn, which is why Juan wasn’t there to battle him. Any future Ever Grande Conference that Ash would be competing in would take place in roughly 9 months.

Question: Is Dazed left or right handed?
Answer: I'd say left-handed!

Question: When she [Dazed] polishes it, does she polish holding it in her left or right hand? [see question 754]
Answer: I figured she'd have her pendulum in her dominant hand since she probably has to keep a tight grip on it with all the force that she channels. Her off hand is mostly used for support, and when she's polishing it she holds the pendulum in her left hand while using the right to rub her mane on it.

Question: You told us where she [Dazed] got the rock for her pendulum from, but where did she get the string from?
Answer: Most Hypno just pluck a few hairs from their mane and cord them into a thread for their pendulum, although I can imagine some preferring to use plant fibers. I doubt any would be too eager to use synthetic materials, although some urban Hypno might out of pragmatism.

Question: We gonna have an orre arc?
Answer: Ash will be visiting Orre at some point, although I haven't hammered out 100% of the details at this time.

Question: Does anyone know if Torrent has any scarring yet?
Answer: Hmm, not that I can remember. He’s taken some hard hits but he’s tough enough that I can’t remember him taking permanent damage.

Question: How tall is Torrent?
Answer: Torrent’s about six feet from tip of his tail to the end of his horns. Throw in about a foot of levitation and he’s usually hovering at seven feet.

Question: How tall is Ash?
Answer: Ash is decently tall for his age. I imagine his build and height will come from Giovanni, who I always pictured to be a big guy.

Question: Does Infernus actually have a tail or is it literally just an area that shoots fire because having your blind spot being on fire seems like a good thing for survival?
Answer: He does have a tail. I figure there is a tail that the flame burns from.

Question: Does your Infernus have a nose of any kind? Or ears that are actually visible?
Answer: I picture Infernus having nostrils more akin to a gorilla than an actual nose. Maybe in that black spot on his face? And haven’t really considered the ears situation! I’m guessing they’re mostly just covered up by the fire

Question: Does Spiritomb actually glow in your depiction, or was that more that when it didn’t manifest it had its presence shown through a purple glow emitting from the keystone.
Answer: Yep. The green orbs have a bit of a sickly green glow, almost like depictions of uranium in cartoons, while the fog drinks up light. When it’s not manifested the purple fog faintly seeps from the Keystone.

Question: How much of Mismagius do you consider to be smoke-like?
Answer: Hmm I mostly imagine the edges to be faintly smoke-like while the majority appears fairly solid. Sort of like the edges are fading away into smoke. That said, I’d love to see your interpretation!

Question: On Battlus.
Answer: I’d recommend Battlus. He’s a true Master and is going to be one of Michael’s most powerful allies. Plus he doesn’t have a criminal past so he’d be more respected on an international stage compared to the rest of Michael’s Elite Four, who will be somewhat sketchy.

Question: On Karen’s team.
Answer: Yep, Absol is a beast. So is Houndoom. She relies heavily on them in physical confrontations, although most of her team is perfectly capable of fighting Distortion resistant teams. Karen isn’t one to allow many weaknesses.

Question: On nurture vs. nature.
Answer: Eh, I’d say there’s room for a well-trained pokemon to take down anything. Some Pokémon have inherent potential or advantages, but training (especially when it comes to shooting Hyper Beams and elemental attacks that equalize the playing field) make things more fair.

Question: On whether there is a year 0.
Answer: I’m not committing to anything until it becomes more relevant in the story, but I do like the idea of a Year 0 covering where Taimu has united Kanto but is organizing for the war against the Drake. Sort of a symbolic acknowledgement that they’d entered a new era, but the job wasn’t quite finished.

Question: In regards to Sidney vs Steven's Metagross, can Metagross prevent any dark type energy from reaching him?
Answer: Metagross is a beast. It and Steven have planned for fighting dark and ghost-types. That said, Sidney is a nightmare for it. Once Metagross is struck - or even just numbed by Distortion saturating the field - Zoroark’s illusions (which are fueled by Distortion) will take effect and even the battle. Generally it takes 2-3 members of the team to safely weaken Metagross (it can usually take one or two down with Miracle Eye and precision shots) for Zoroark to safely take it down. That’s absolutely crippling when you still have five Champion-levels to fight. Sidney is very, very well-suited to nullifying Steven’s biggest advantage though.

Question: How does Mega Aerodactyl stack up to a Mega Metagross?
Answer: And I’d definitely give it to Mega Metagross!

Question: Does Sidney have Mega Sharpedo?
Answer: Generally you can assume that an Elite Four member will have any Mega Stone available to their team!

Question: Can Regular Sharpedo float like Kingdra?
Answer: They probably could with specialized use of Distortion, but it’d be draining.

Question: On the Sensational Sisters.
Answer: The Sisters are actually really good at their job. Similar to Erica in that they do specialize more in peacetime, but they’re also much more willing to be aggressive and drop the hammer when needed.

Question: On Giovanni’s starter.
Answer: I always figured Persian was his starter.

Question: On Giovanni’s Persian.
Answer: Yep. Giovanni’s Persian was Champion-level, similar to Michael’s Jolteon.

Question: What does a champion level Persian look like? In comparison to Jessica's.
Answer: Not too dissimilar, honestly. It’s definitely more muscular but not by too much. The biggest difference is just it’s sheer control and mastery over techniques.

Question: On Kanto’s topography.
Answer: Kanto has a lot of woods in general. Central Kanto is definitely flatter and more developed, so forests have been pushed back around the cities. Celadon has a lot of farmland. Otherwise I don’t have too many specifics to add!

Question: On the name of the planet.
Answer: I generally just call it Earth. I’ve had some groups in Sinnoh (usually Legendary cults) call the planet Coronet, but that’s because they’re Sinnoh-centric and wrapped up in their own mythology.

Question: Thinking about writing about the beginning of the National League, who were the Champions at that time?
Answer: Hoenn: Drake.Indigo: Uther.Sinnoh: Irene.

Question: On whether part of Ash’s team can draw power from his Feather.
Answer: Theoretically it's possible for Infernus, Oz, and Sneasel to draw upon the Feather's power, but it would be much more difficult for them than a psychic. It would definitely require direct contact, which could mean it would be difficult to use in a life-or-death battle. Psychics kind of have a built-in advantage where their abilities are much more tuned for drawing on ambient power and directing it.

Question: Psychics are overall pretty OP, huh?
Answer: Oh yeah, their sheer versatility makes them utter nightmares. They were even scarier before TMs became available and pretty much everything could learn at least one technique to pierce through psychic powers.

Question: Were Alakazams on the level of Dragonite and Salamence originally? [see question 781]
Answer: Sort of. Dragonite and Salamence tend to be more powerful and dangerous in a straight fight, but nothing wants to fight an Alakazam if they can help it. It's not worth it, especially since most Alakazam can easily just deal damage, teleport away, and then chip away at you for the next few days. They're impossible to beat in battles of attrition, so they were given a wide berth.

Question: Which was more feared alakazam or gengar? [see question 781]
Answer: Probably Gengar. Alakazam generally aren't hostile, although there's definitely exceptions.

Question: What's the best defense against a gengar?
Answer: Probably a dark-type, something that could put a little fear into the Gengar and keep it from being as aggressive.

Question: Were Dusknoirs around too or were they so reclusive no one had one back in the day?
Answer: Dusknoir were around, but they usually functioned as guardians of places. Very unusual to see one loyal to a human - they were very respected, but neutral.

Question: On Torrent’s future defense.
Answer: He'll [Torrent will] be working towards more specialized forms of defense later on, but currently I will say that a water shield would be able to intercept most elemental attacks - it requires a water source, however.

Question: On Ditto’s common battling strategy.
Answer: So, for Traveler, I have Ditto as able to store DNA samples that they can shift into. They degrade fairly quickly (especially when they’re used) so it’s more common to just keep Ditto stocked with the rest of your team’s DNA. It gives it additional versatility, and that experience fighting in a variety of forms make them more adept at taking on opponent’s forms of all body types.

Question: Are the armor parts of steel/rock types organic?
Answer: It depends on the Pokémon, but many rock and steel types have primarily inorganic shells with some organic components. It would be a little difficult to get DNA from the shell and armor; but not entirely impossible. It’s just more practical to get DNA from the squishy bits.

Question: Does Traveler!Ash see Daisy in any kind of sisterly/relative way or do you think he does/could appreciate her as an attractive female?
Answer: Good question! Ash views Daisy as something between an older sister/cousin sort of way and a friend. That said, Ash does think she’s pretty. Because of both his age and the way he perceives her it’s a complete afterthought, though.

Question: On weapons.
Answer: Basically, guns don’t exist to the point we have them in real life. The technology exists, sure, but nothing more advanced than primitive firearms were really developed. The problem arises in that more emphasis was placed on heavy weapons like cannons that could feasibly hurt a Pokémon - most can easily shrug off a few bullets, and early weaponry was so primitive and unwieldy that it just never took off. It wasn’t accurate enough to reliably hit a pokemon, it tended to really piss off and unsettled wild Pokémon, it made humans a much bigger target in engagements (whereas they were mostly ignored by Pokémon before), and psychics had a nasty habit of forcing people with guns to shoot at their teammates through mind control, physical manipulation, or just making the human hallucinate. Not to mention psychic barriers can pretty easily soak up bullets, especially from primitive, undeveloped firearms.

Question: On Gary’s Pidgeot.
Answer: Yep, Gary’s Pidgeot is naturally a giant of its species. Gary leaned into that and basically trained it as a dragon slayer - big and bulky enough to match or overpower most dragons and faster too. [see question 152]

Question: How limited is torrent on land again?
Answer: Torrent’s limited to roughly 2/3 of his true strength while on land!

Question: Does battling on a field with water pools count as on land? Or is he [Torrent] as strong in those pools as he would be at sea or on a fully submerged battlefield? [see question 792]
Answer: The water pools count as long as he’s in them - just reducing his need to levitate lets him focus his strength on battling. His raw offensive power is going to increase depending on how much water he has available, especially with water manipulation.

Question: Do you think Dragonite are disadvantaged underwater?
Answer: Underwater, yes. I see Dragonite as being comfortable in water, but they lose a lot of their mobility underwater compared to when they were Dratini and Dragonair.

Question: How strong is Dov compared to Lance's main Dragonite?
Answer: Dog’s more physical and brute force focused. Heavier but not as quick and nimble in the air.

Question: Am I correct in thinking that Lance’s average Pokémon is stronger than Cynthia’s average Pokémon, but Dragonite is weaker than Princess?
Answer: Lance’s average Pokémon is stronger than Cynthia’s, and I’d say Dragonite and Princess are equally matched. Dragonite just has an advantage because of flight. If Dragonite is grounded then Princess has a large advantage, though.

Question: Is Lance's main dragonite related to Dov?
Answer: To be honest, I can’t fully remember...I want to say that Dragonite and Dov are related somehow? It’s one of those things buried in my notes.

Question: Do Lance’s Dragonite know Barrier?
Answer: Of course! They also evolved several levels early. Breaking game mechanics is Lance’s real trump card.

Question: Is Magnus X or Y, or does Lance have both?
Answer: Will confirm Lance has both.

Question: On Giovanni’s strength.
Answer: Giovanni is one of those characters who would hit Master-level easy if he put his mind to it. He probably could’ve been Champion if he invested his energy into it.

Question: Is Oak at his current level weaker than Agatha?
Answer: It’s a sticky situation for Oak vs. Agatha. I know in the story that I said Oak could still take her, but as things have become more developed and fleshed out I say it’s a toss up. Agatha is in better shape, but much of Oak’s team still regularly trains (albeit without his input) and it’s not unheard of for League members to come by for a friendly spar.

Question: Could Giovanni alone and unregulated but with prep take down Oak, if Oak has equal prep?
Answer: Yeah, no way he takes down Oak on his own, especially with equal amounts of prep. Oak is several tiers higher.

Question: Who would be willing to give Ash The Talk? I'm more interested in the trust aspect of this question.
Answer: If purely hypothetical, I’d say plenty of people would be willing if necessary: Professor Oak, Lance, Steven, Karen, etc. Definitely a few who would do it just for the entertainment value - Agatha would have a fun time with that.

Question: Do y’all think AZ and Floette are still alive in Traveler, or do you think they got ganked by one of the Aura Trio?
Answer: I will confirm they’re alive!

Question: On Bruiser’s Rampage current strength [around chapter 57].
Answer: Yep, his current sustainable Rampage is close to his original interpretation. Just more experience and time for his body to build up to it.

Question: How recent is cloned meat?
Answer: Really in the last 10-15 years I’d say. It’s had the proof of concept done since Blaine’s first forays into cloning, but the mass synthesizing of meat wasn’t practical until Ditto cells became accessible. Solutions with ditto cells were able to make it much, much easier to cultivate.

Question: What's the official name for the unaffiliated traveling masters?
Answer: The official term is Nomad!

Question: Now what is making Ash so interesting to the legends, especially to the point of branding him?
Answer: One thing I will say is that Branding is mostly a passive process. Just being in a Legend’s unfiltered presence leaves a mark. Mewtwo didn’t leave much of a Brand while in the armor, but his encounter with Articuno marked him and altered him, as did his future encounters with the Birds.

Question: But don’t the concepts stem from the Bands that the legends have on him?
Answer: Yup, Brands come from Concepts, not the other way around!

Question: How does Wattson have a mega stone?
Answer: Yep, Watson’s journey to Kalos was part of it, along with him being highly trusted by the Ever Grande League.

Question: How does Leon square up as a Champion compared to the others?
Answer: Leon’s genuinely Champion-level. I’d put him around Wallace’s level. His problem is that due to Galar’s isolation he hasn’t had the opportunities to fight against many other Champion-level opponents.

Question: Was he [Wattson] part of the Elite 4?
Answer: Wattson was not part of the Elite Four actually! Content as a Gym Leader.

Question: What does Leon specialise in?
Answer: Similar to what’s above, I see Leon as specializing a bit in destructive force. Not so much dragons as whatever works. I do think he favors the trickery of the ghost-type in part.

Question: So unless Lance had to go full out in an emergency (though begs the question why he didn't [use Mega Evolution] in Viridian?
Answer: Most likely the certainty of exposure. If they were out in the wilderness he absolutely would’ve unleashed his full firepower with Megas.

Question: Is Will in the fourth seat or Koga?
Answer: Ah, I will say that currently the Indigo Elite Four rankings are Will, then Karen, then Koga, then Bruno. My view of it is that Gold and Silver take place several years after Red and Blue, so they have time to grow into the roles.

Question: Given the time passed that Ash is now 12, does that mean those 2 [May and Brendan] have now started their journeys in the background?
Answer: Yep, Ash had his twelfth birthday shortly before he left for Hoenn! It’s currently September and I’m planning to have the Ever Grande Conference in May, so the new trainers will start in June.

Question: In the Pokemon world where there are many species smarter, stronger and more mobile than humans, how did we end up in a world dominated by humans?
Answer: Largely humanity’s ability to organize. Similar to our world, their lack of natural physical power put them at an inherent disadvantage that they compensated for by organizing and cooperating with other humans as well as Pokemon - until psychics and Aura-users advanced to the point humanity could first begin to communicate with wild Pokémon, humanity was on the verge of extinction.

Question: The Pokemon with the capability to develop civilization didn't have the temperament or desire to, and the Pokemon that were more social didn't have the capability? [see question 817]
Answer: Yep! Pokémon have relatively easy lives outside of competing with one another. They’re born with incredible natural gifts - humanity were turned out into the world with little more than intelligence and good social skills lol.

Question: On human psychics over time.
Answer: I will say that psychics appear at roughly the same rate nowadays as they did previously. With humanity’s larger population there’s actually more psychics - I think the thing is that most are very low-level compared to monsters like Sabrina, or even skilled users like Will.

Question: On the King Under the Mountains strength.
Answer: The King alone is probably worth an entire region’s Gym Leaders.

Question: Does the Sinnoh Underground Exist in Traveler?
Answer: Yep, the Underground still exists in a way. I’ve expanded it into the labyrinthine network of tunnels that extends out from Mt. Coronet.

Question: How long ago did Fino Moore retire from the Hoenn Elite 4?
Answer: I figured it would be relatively recent. Last 5-10 years most likely!

Question: What's his [Marcus’] team actually?
Answer: Marcus was a Rock Master, and Uther who came before was a Normal Master.

Question: On Pierce’s team.
Answer: Pierce had a fun habit of basically collecting the Pokémon equivalent of serial killers and then adding them to his team.

Question: In most of the history, most every Trainer/Guardian would have been nobility, due to the requirements of feeding and keeping pkmn.
Answer: Yep, in ancient times pretty much anyone who had the resources to feed and support a battling Pokémon had to be nobility. Plenty of Pokémon partners out there, but it was difficult and resource intensive to train one up to be a powerful battler.

Question: On Chinatsu’s strength.
Answer: Chinatsu’s at the level where she could slaughter Lance’s team. We haven’t seen her capabilities, but she’s pretty much impossible to kill and one hit from her is lethal.

Question: On trainers capable of beating the Elite Four.
Answer: The problem there is that the number of trainers who can beat the entire Elite Four are so rare that they’re pretty much already Champions.

Question: On what Giovanni expected regarding Mewtwo’s strength.
Answer: Giovanni expected Mewtwo to be at the level of anime!Mewtwo, similar to how Tobias expected Darkrai to be at the level of anime!Darkrai.

Question: Did Gio have a plan to control Anime level Mewtwo that was better than what he tried in the anime?
Answer: Yes, it’s something that I can’t spoil yet but Giovanni would have been completely safe from anime!Mewtwo.

Question: It was mentioned the Indigo Conference was in March, and based on in story discussions then it can be assumed the Silver & Ever Grande conferences are in April, correct? Are other conferences around the same time of year?
Answer: Ah, I set it up to where it’s basically in the order of the generations. Indigo in March, Silver in April, Ever Grande in May, etc. [see question 182]

Question: How common are Eevee in Traveler?
Answer: More common than the starters, I think. I see them as pretty rare, but not in the realm of something like a Dratini. It’s feasible for the average trainer to pick one up if they put the work in or pay the right price.

Question: Have we seen a female starter?
Answer: Will confirm that female starters aren’t banned for trainers!

Question: Is the game's gender ratio present in Traveler (what, 87.5% for starters, eevee, a few other favourites)?
Answer: Yep, those ratios still exist. It isn’t something I take into account when introducing members of each species, however.

Question: On starters.
Answer: I will say that although starters are rare, it is plenty possible to get them as a trainer. Some families might get it for a rookie trainer and you can acquire them other places as well. Rare, but not unheard of. Just difficult to find in the wild.

Question: Do starters come from a particular breeder or do Professors breed their own?
Answer: I figure there’s probably a variety of breeders for each starter!

Question: It was mentioned the Indigo Conference was in March, and based on in story discussions then it can be assumed the Silver & Ever Grande conferences are in April, correct? Are other conferences around the same time of year?
Answer: Ah, I set it up to where it’s basically in the order of the generations. Indigo in March, Silver in April, Ever Grande in May, etc.

Question: So Lily June, Vertress July? [see question 836]
Answer: Yep!

Question: How common are Eevee in Traveler? As in, as rare as the starters? Or perhaps a bit more rare than that?
Answer: More common than the starters, I think. I see them as pretty rare, but not in the realm of something like a Dratini. It’s feasible for the average trainer to pick one up if they put the work in or pay the right price.

Question: That makes me wonder, are female starters banned for use for trainers?
Answer: Will confirm that female starters aren’t banned for trainers!

Question: Is the games’ gender ratio present in Traveler (what, 87.5% for starters, eevee, a few other favourites)?
Answer: Another mistake. The question is the same as 833. This time I’ll use the opportunity to proclaim that I belong to the “Tangrowth is best ‘mon” supremacy.

Question: On how rare starters are.
Answer: I will say that although starters are rare, it is plenty possible to get them as a trainer. Some families might get it for a rookie trainer and you can acquire them other places as well. Rare, but not unheard of. Just difficult to find in the wild.

Question: Do starters come from a particular breeder or do Professors breed their own?
Answer: I figure there’s probably a variety of breeders for each starter!

Question: On Ash vs. Lance as of chapter 56.
Answer: To add to the debate, I will drop a reminder that Ash went 2-6 with Lance offscreen. A very small mention in...56 I believe.

Question: Which 2 pokemon did Ash beat off of Lance's team?
Answer: I figure Lance would use one of his Dragonite and then most likely Mael as a challenge for Torrent.

Question: The thing is does he [Lance] even use flying techniques apart from well flying?
Answer: He definitely uses things like Hurricane - Lance is really the type of trainer to just use whatever works best!

Question: On Professor Oak’s team.
Answer: I will say that all living members of Professor Oak’s team can be found at the end of 55.

Question: Has Lysandre ever been arrested for crimes against hair?
Answer: No, he’s actually considered to be the most fashionable man in Kalos.

Question: Do Life Orbs even exist in Traveler?
Answer: A Life Orb I can see as an Aura-forged enhancement from ancient Sinnoh. Probably won’t show up in the story, but it’s the most reasonable thing for a “this makes you stronger, but slowly kills you” item.

Question: On the amount of champions a Legendary can have.
Answer: I will say that Legendaries are able to have multiple mortal champions!

Question: If I was isekai’d into Traveler how would I go about becoming a legendary champion? And who’s champion would I want to be?
Answer: Theoretically, I’d say Moltres is the easiest and with the least amount of drawbacks. If you became a trainer, embodied the traits like courage, strength, and determination that Moltres likes, and sought it out then there’s a decent chance it would see you as a good vessel for its power.

Question: Who would win, Clefable or Chinatsu?
Answer: Clefable if Chinatsu doesn’t use the Feather to boost her abilities, Chinatsu if she does.

Question: Rereading 56 and I was wondering how apartment 151 became 201?
Answer: Ah, if I remember correctly that’s just a detail I forgot to correct lol.

Question: Is Dazed just used to it [the Brands’ influence] or because she is also indirectly touched?
Answer: Yep, she’s adjusted. She’s had secondhand exposure to many Legends, and also has grown accustomed to the power and pressure rolling off Ash.

Question: Does that also make Dazed stronger or advance faster since she has to constantly deal with the pressure coming off Ash? And does eating Ash’s Nightmares affect Dazed’s growth, or does it just taste bad?
Answer: Nope, it doesn’t help Dazed grow in raw power. [Redacted] has it right. Dazed is uniquely suited for dealing with mental juggernauts like Legends (well, standing conscious against them) which will go a long way in making her an incredibly formidable mental opponent as she hones that part of her skill set going forward.

Question: On Torrent’s age.
Answer: Yep, Torrent spent...maybe 2-3 months as a Seadra, if that. Crazy in retrospect.

Question: Is Ash's team practicing teamwork? I know there's Infernus/Tangrowth and Infernus/Oz, but I don't know if the focus is more on preparing the field rather than helping each other during a battle.
Answer: Yep, Ash has been practicing teamwork. It’s still not his primary focus, but Steven’s been working it in...plus the Rocket raid made him realize that was a weak point.

Question: Is Ash the first person in recent memory that actually pushed Michael that hard?
Answer: I’d say Greevil definitely pushed Michael harder - Greevil was a proper Master, and the only reason Michael won was because of XD001 being snagged.

Question: How would Wes have fared against Greevil, minus XD001?
Answer: I think Wes would have beaten Greevil handily, but it would come down to a difference in attitude. Wes would go straight for the kill, and without Greevil directing his shadows they would lose all cohesion and be easy pickings.

Question: Greevil was master level with his shadows or before the shadows are counted?
Answer: With the Shadows!

Question: Have you thought about the lore behind Pokemon that can change their typing like Meloetta?
Answer: Yep, I’ve put some thought into it. The main one I’ve thought about is Shaymin, but I have plans for the others.

Question: Has it been mentioned anywhere why exactly Daisy did this disappearing trick on the Oak family in this setting?
Answer: Simple familial dysfunction. Daisy would rather stay away from the Oak’s rather than confront that pain with her grandfather. Gardevoir certainly isn’t a good influence on her in that respect.

Question: Do you think Gardevoir subtly talks Daisy out of going back whenever she has a moment of weakness or misses her family ? [see question 861]
Answer: That’s exactly it. Gardevoir wants to spare Daisy the pain... even if confronting that is ultimately more healthy for her.

Question: How does Drake compare to Alder? I know Drake was a former champion, so how does he compare to Alder?
Answer: While both are definitely Champion-Level trainers, like [redacted] previously stated, by nature of dragons having longevity, Drake retained most of his strength, while Alder's team waned with age.

Question: Does the red genesect exist? And if so, how does it stack up to the others?
Answer: Yeah, Red Genesect exists. It's not necessarily more powerful than the other Genesects, but it's sort of specialized to act as a leader to the others in combat scenarios.

Question: Does this mean they [Beast trio] are as immortal as demigods, i.e. immortal in the sense that they can still die or does it simply imply that they were once mortal before being turned into legends?
Answer: The latter. The Beasts cannot die.

Question: How rare are Feebas in Traveler?
Answer: In Sinnoh they're limited to water pools found underneath Coronet - recall the conversation Gary and Samuel had about Daisy's. They don't breed well in captivity either. In Hoenn they're limited to springs found at the headwaters of some of Hoenn's rivers.

Question: How rare are Beldum colonies and where would one likely be in regions like Unova and Sinnoh?
Answer: Extremely rare - I'm sure there are a couple over on the Western Continent and whatnot and probably some others on the Eastern but none that are really under the control of the Leagues there, I think. Hoenn's pretty unique in that the Stones have actually formed a working relationship with the local colony. Their situation is pretty unique in that way.

Question: Does the Lucky Egg or Exp Share exist in Traveler? If so, how does it work?
Answer: No, those are too gamey.

Question: How about the choice items? [see question 868]
Answer: Choice items also aren't a thing. Pokemon don't really hold items in Traveler's battles.

Question: Can electric types redirect other electric moves?
Answer: Yes, recall Amelia's Raichu and Oz playing catch with electric charges.

Question: How do Everstones work in Traveler anyway?
Answer: Much like Fire Stones or Water Stones impart a spark of Fire and Water energy/concept onto those who come into contact with them, Everstones have a kind of 'static' concept. They enforce same-ness and, to an extent, stagnation.

Question: Is Ash's team practicing teamwork? I know there's Infernus/Tangrowth and Infernus/Oz, but I don't know if the focus is more on preparing the field rather than helping each other during a battle.
Answer: Yep, Ash has been practicing teamwork. It’s still not his primary focus, but Steven’s been working it in...plus the Rocket raid made him realize that was a weak point.

Question: Is Ash the first person in recent memory that actually pushed Michael that hard? I feel like Greevil pushed Michael hard, but did Ash push harder?
Answer: And I’d say Greevil definitely pushed Michael harder - Greevil was a proper Master, and the only reason Michael won was because of XD001 being snagged.

Question: How would Wes have fared against Greevil, minus XD001?
Answer: I think Wes would have beaten Greevil handily, but it would come down to a difference in attitude. Wes would go straight for the kill, and without Greevil directing his shadows they would lose all cohesion and be easy pickings.

Question: Greevil was master level with his shadows or before the shadows are counted?
Answer: With the Shadows!

Question: On how Dazed’s pendulum affects her powers.
Answer: I will say that the sway of Dazed’s pendulum affects her hypnosis, but not more physical powers.

Question: On how Ash uses Infernus.
Answer: One thing to remember is that pretty much every battle during Johto (minus Cynthia and Karen) was a vacation for Ash. He was there to have fun in a low-stakes battle and indulge himself and his team. He did take Clair seriously and wanted to win, but the way he behaved in that fight is very different to how he’d behave in something like the Conference or a real fight.

Question: Would a pokemon like Tyranitar that used to be a ground type before evolving retain that electrical resistance or would it have lost that when it became a dark type?
Answer: I don’t think it would in most cases, which I’ll attribute to the change in physiology due to the evolution. That said, given how thick and durable a Tyranitar’s armor is, I don’t foresee most electric-type attacks being able to penetrate it regardless.

Question: What color is Bruiser's skin? is it Purple or is it Grey?
Answer: I’d say it’s more grey than purple!

Question: On Leon’s potential.
Answer: As for Leon, I’d say he has relative Lance-level potential, but is somewhat crippled by Galar’s policy of isolationism and lack of high-level competition (or at least the same diversity you can find elsewhere).

Question: On Reshiram and Zekrom’s champions.
Answer: Yep, Reshiram and Zekrom are interesting in that they are extremely powerful, right along with Lugia or Ho-Oh as comparisons, but act almost exclusively through mortal champions that they empower.

Question: Do they [Reshiram and Zekrom] have high intelligence?
Answer: I’d say so. Intelligence is hard to quantify with Legends since they’re so in-tune with the universe and their own nature above all else, but I’d definitely say that Reshiram and Zekrom have more of a ‘human’ intelligence if that makes sense.

Question: What happens when a legend “dies”?
Answer: Yep, if a Legend’s physical form is destroyed, they’re able to pretty much immediately reconstitute themselves. Their corporeal form is just an expression of their Concept - even if their body is destroyed, that isn’t affecting the underlying Concept.

Question: The concepts try to interfere with each other’s concepts when they try to battle them?
Answer: Pretty much! The battles are as much spiritual as physical. Although in the end those are one and the same.

Question: Is there a difference between say Zapdos and Zekrom? Or are they the same Concept, just that one has a larger infinity?
Answer: They have similar ways their power manifest, but it’s derived from different Concepts.

Question: So, uh, what was up with Ash threatening Moltres at Shamouti? [see question 883]
Answer: That Third of Moltres was about as close to its corporeal form being destroyed as possible, and Zapdos and Articuno had ripped it apart metaphysically as well. Could Ash kill it? Absolutely not.

Question: If the Clefable from Mount Moon had to fight Lavender, how close would it be?
Answer: Definitely Lavender in this matchup. It’s ridiculously strong, and its scale is just too much for Clefable to beat. That said, Clefable could hurt Lavender like no other pseudo-Legendary we’ve seen, although it isn’t capable of actually destroying Lavender.

Question: Interesting that you mention we've seen, implying there's probably a dark or another ghost of similar scale that can wreck Lavender? Also does this mean Chinatsu doesn't stand up to its scale either? [see question 887]
RelatedTags: ['Chinatsu', 'Dark type', 'Ghost of Lavender', 'Ghost type', 'Moltres', 'Pokemon Battle', 'Pseudo Legendary']
Answer: I will confirm that there are other pseudo-Legendaries with the capability of killing Lavender, although it would be incredibly close and a Pyrrhic victory at best. There’s nothing outside the Legends that could outright dominate Lavender.

Question: On Palkia.
Answer: Yep, omnipresence is a good way to describe how Palkia acts in Traveler.

Question: Is it explained somewhere on how exactly teleportation works in Traveler?
Answer: It is essentially ripping a hole in spacetime and hurling yourself through it.

Question: On the Lugia and the Legendary Birds/ Ho-Oh and the Legendary Beasts.
Answer: There are 9 Birds in total - 3 of each. There are only 3 Beasts, each unique.

Question: I noticed the emphasis on Dazed's names for the others, with Ash being Friend-Trainer. However, in Chapter 23, she calls him Trainer-Friend. Is there a significance in the change, or is it just something Elf might have not noticed and overlooked?
Answer: Nope, it’s a good question! That change was intentional. I believe she also calls him Trainer-Friend in Chapter 20 when she first develops her telepathy.

Question: On the Crystal Onix.
RelatedTags: ['Crystal Onix', 'King Under the Mountain', 'Onix', 'Orange Islands', 'Pokemon Biology']
Answer: I have the crystal Onix as a subspecies native to the Orange Islands, while the King is a standard Onix.

Question: What does Shinobu's name mean? On that note what does Chinatsu mean?
Answer: Shinobu means ‘Endure’, and Chinatsu means ‘One Thousand Summers’.

Question: What does Infernus call his teammates?
Answer: Referring to the Infernus discussion, he thinks of his teammates as the names Ash has given them - rather than the more esoteric names that Dazed and many others think of them as - mainly as a reflection of his respect for Ash.

Question: Do the others also refer to each other as their names they picked? Or are they like Dazed and her Friend-Trainer names? [see question 895]
Answer: It pretty much comes down to the individual and how they perceive the world!

Question: Tangrowth is seemingly unable to remember the name Ash had once given him, so I don't think he actually remembers any names.
Answer: Why do you think he’s always trying to touch things?[]

Question: I thought he was half-blind or something.
Answer: He pretty much is! Throw in his, uh, cognitive skills and I like to imagine he mostly just remembers who people are through touch.

Question: How long have TMs existed in the Pokemon Universe? From a technological standpoint we know Pokeballs were brought into the world by the classic Professor, but I'm wondering if TMs were a later invention? Also were Apricorn Balls invented before Samuel made any?
Answer: 1. Yes, you can refer to the Sevii Islands chapter - I believe Ash describes one of the reasons the Sevii Islands not having as good a connection to their wild pokemon being that they didn't have apricorns like the mainland, or something like that. Suffice to say they existed as the proto-pokeball for a while, or they function as artisan pokeballs. Idk how up to date that is in the canon of Traveler since we more or less know they didn't exist during Taimu's time but it's there to look at.

Question: Was it stated that Infernus's heat can even affect ghosts when they fade?
Answer: For ghosts, many are capable of briefly phasing into the Distortion World for an instant or two at a time in order to avoid attacks. Sustained assaults like a Flamethrower are useful against ghosts because very few can phase long enough to avoid it, while a simple punch (without any additional energy or elements put into it) can be phased through without much trouble.

Question: Do Pokémon need to love battle in order to attain higher tiers, or does love for battle play a very small part. Like could a Pokémon like Nidoking get as strong as e4 on just loyalty and training alone, or does the lack of drive make it nearly impossible without the use of drugs (like Giovanni’s Persian)?
Answer: A love of battle isn’t necessary to be great, but I would say there needs to be some strong motivation in another area that pushes them along.

Question: Why has Daisy not evolved Eevee? Does she want it to stay an Eevee, or something else? Also, what’s the opinion on making a Pokémon not Evolve like Clair’s Dragonair?
Answer: For Eevee, I have it as one of those situations where the Pokémon itself is perfectly fine staying in its unevolved state.

Question: If an eevee came into contact with Suicune, would they evolve into a vaporeon?
Answer: Yep, I’d absolutely say so.

Question: Do Ghosts get the same boost that everyone else does by being close to something like an opening to the Distortion world, or Giratina? Because it feels like they would be linked by default in a similar way as Ash to Mewtwo since they were once a part of Giratina/Mewtwo at one point. But at the same time I don't know if they would have it permanently, and thus have no boost otherwise. Can Ghosts call upon this relationship for a large boost in power? Do they risk unravelling into Distortion by doing so?
RelatedTags: ['Ash', 'Distortion', 'Distortion World', 'Ghost type', 'Giratina', 'Lavender Town', 'Legendary Pokemon', 'Mewtwo', 'Mt. Pyre', 'Pokemon Biology']
Answer: Yep, although it’s a bit of a double-edged sword. Ghosts can draw on the latent Distortional energies and influence of places like Lavender Town or Mt. Pyre in order to enhance their own abilities and strengthen themselves, but it tends to be temporary and overdrawing will cause instability in their corporeal forms. They’re essentially limited by the amount of Distortion they can safely handle. The stronger the ghost, the more potent they are in those environments.

Question: When you say ghosts can turn it into a permanent boost, do you mean that they can permanently boost the max Distortion that they can channel? Or do you mean something else? [see question 904]
Answer: Pretty much. It basically trains their bodies to grow more accustomed to larger amounts of Distortion.

Question: Because the Ghost becomes reliant on the area for that strength, that means that Lavender is more or less restricted to Lavender Town unless it was willing to lose it's massive power?
Answer: Lavender’s in the odd situation where it settled in the valley because it was a bit more open to Distortion, but at this point it’s converted the entire valley and city into its ‘body’. Theoretically it could uproot the land itself and move, weakening the barriers between the material and Distortion wherever it went, but that would also raise its chances of being hunted by Cresselia.

Question: Who is technically stronger, the Alakazam line or the Gardevoir line?
Answer: Yep, I’d say they’re equal. Different species have different specialties, which may or may not prove to be more immediately useful than other psychic species.

Question: Will Elf adapt Movie 4 through infinity?
Answer: Pretty much! After the third movie, the timeline’s changed enough to where most movies won’t happen like they did in the anime. Their influences will still be felt in the world, though.

Question: I was looking at question 660, and I noticed something. It says Drake is probably the strongest of any Elite 4, does that mean he could beat Lance? Or is he not Champion level?
Answer: Ah, Lance is superior to Drake. That said, Drake is still Champion level with his team largely in their prime (advantages of training dragons) and can overwhelm most Elite Four easily.

Question: How would you describe the difference between his [Drake’s] and Lance's teams/levels?
Answer: Don’t want to get too much into that at this point, but I will say that while Lance focuses on speed, maneuverability, and ranged attacks, Drake focuses on the physical might and endurance of dragons.

Question: How much damage could Ash do before the League could stop him?
Answer: Ash is at the level to where he could do serious damage and steamroll large groups of lesser trainers, but isn’t strong enough at this point to resist a League squad.

Question: On the reach of Mewtwo’s power.
Answer: Yep, Mewtwo can basically project power anywhere in the world. It grows more difficult with distance, but Mewtwo is capable of exerting godlike force pretty much anywhere on the globe.

Question: Could he [Mewtwo] still [affect] Ash at a large distance if Sneasel pumped distortion in him like at Greenfield? [see question 912]
Answer: Pretty much any amount of Distortion produced by a mortal Pokémon is insignificant to Mewtwo. He could even burn away the Ghost of Lavender with just a few minutes of effort. Just massive gaps in power.

Question: I remember reading that Sidney’s Zoroark was ‘dripping darkness’ or whatever that meant. Like what does that actually mean?
Answer: Basically just imagine globs of pitch black sludge dripping from Zoroark’s fur. Purely an illusory effect that Zoroark keeps up just to be a drama queen.

Question: What was Mamoru's herd doing around Celadon/Saffron? What reason do they or any rhydon herd have to migrate around like that?
Answer: Herds regularly migrate around for resources, acquiring mates, training, etc. Mamoru also makes a point to make a tour of the region to purge potential rivals and remind everyone who’s boss.

Question: What exactly is stopping Mewtwo from rezzing Ambertwo?
Answer: Mewtwo has no access to her biological remains, her Aura, or access to anyone who would have remembered the original Amber... on account of him killing her father.

Question: Does Jirachi only have a local effect? Or does it make all of those around the world pursue their desires and dreams similar to ice types being boosted by Articuno?
Answer: As for Jirachi, it would have a much wider effect normally, even during hibernation. At least enough to drive the desires of those within a few hours, which waxes the closer it is to waking.

Question: How widespread would it be unmuted? [see question 917]
Answer: I’d say that it would expand to at least Lavaridge and Fallarbor, but probably wouldn’t have a significant effect on Rustboro or Mauville.

Question: Do Archie and Maxie have their R/S/E uniforms or their OR/AS uniforms?
Answer: ORAS uniforms for the most part.

Question: Was Mamoru really big, or just big by Rhydon standards? I thought he was Rhyperior sized?
Answer: For reference, Mamoru stands a good two to three feet higher than the average Rhyperior.

Question: How prominent are the top Coordinators in terms of the public eye?
Answer: I’ll chip in and say that Coordinators are a big deal. They and tournament trainers are two sides of the same coin. There is significant overlap, but top-tier Coordinators and top-tier trainers tend to be distinct - one of the reasons Wallace is such a big deal.

Question: Is Wallace unique in walking both roads then. I mean to his level it seems like it, pinnacle of both. But are there many that do that?
Answer: Nope! Even Daisy manages to be a high-level combat trainer even if she doesn’t like it. Wallace has just taken it further than most. The average trainers tend to be average at both, but the high-level coordinators still manage to be decent combatants for the most part.

Question: On the Draconids.
Answer: So, this is something I've been poking elf about, in an effort to create an architectural style and overall design for the draconids, and it's been really fascinating.

Question: How did the Wataru spread to this generation if they married inside their family? Wouldn’t inbreeding eventually make that an impossibility to create more offspring?
Answer: One thing to remember is that the Wataru are massive to the point that their clan compound is basically its own little town. Plenty of branch families, and it was fairly common to marry unrelated people from Blackthorn or political marriages with other noble families throughout Indigo to bring new blood in. You wouldn't see Targaryen-style inbreeding except in rare cases. It was pretty common to see first cousins marrying, but that's also been common throughout human history.

Question: How do Jinx have pre-evolutions if they were humans changed by Hakuea?
Answer: Jinx who are turned by Haukea are formed into Jinx directly. Second generation Jinx and beyond go through the Smoochum phase.

Question: How did they work with Pokémon back then without Pokéball is the wonder?
Answer: Psychics and Aura-users enabled the first bonds with pokemon to be formed, and after that it turned into a self-perpetuating partnership.

Question: How does the Snag Machine work?  Does it override pokeball registrations?  Does it only work because Orre is a lawless hellhole and no one really tracks Pokemon ownership the same way as in Kanto?
Answer: Yeah it’s one of those things where there’s really no excuse for it unless the Pokémon is absolutely desperate or protecting itself. Sapience makes things simultaneously more and less complicated on that front.

Question: On the requirements to challenge a region’s Champion.
Answer: You have to be part of a specific League’s Elite Four to challenge for the actual position. Most Champions would be happy to do exhibition battles and stuff, but having a random member of another League challenge for the Champion title introduces a lot of problems.

Question: Has any member of an elite 4 or champion ever had someone use an exhibition or challenger match as an assassination attempt on them or their pokemon?
Answer: Nope, in a regulated setting like an exhibition match deaths are pretty much unheard of. Too much security, too many eyes watching, and too much access to medical care. Even if a fatal blow was dealt, odds are the Pokémon could be brought back.

Question: How late is too late in the pokemon world? Brain death?
Answer: Pretty much. Their medical abilities are absolutely insane. Honestly, so much as the brain isn’t outright destroyed in battle I can imagine you’d be able to bring a Pokémon back (albeit with a ton of resources invested).

Question: On Plume’s moveset.
Answer: She does have Air Slash! I don’t believe so [she doesn’t have Feather Dance].

Question: Does Infernus have Solar Beam?
Answer: One of those things where he has it, but has no need for it really.

Question: On how hard it is to learn Blast Burn.
Answer: To address some of the questions above, Blast Burn is learnable by all fire-types in Traveler but does not exist in TM form. It’s a closely guarded secret of Fire Masters, and only taught to those they hold in high regard (or who pay a sufficient amount). Most trainers and Pokémon would be unable to use it even if taught. Mastering even the first stages shows a high degree of both skill and determination.

Question: So theoretically squirtle hydro cannon is a go?
Answer: Yep.

Question: Is he [Lucian] a psychic in Traveler?
Answer: Lucian isn’t psychic in Traveler. I can see him training his potential just enough to make it easier for him to contact, but he doesn’t have the potential to become someone like Will.

Question: Couldn’t he give himself 20/20 vision with his Psychic powers?
Answer: He easily could! With his level of psychic power very little is beyond him. There’s really no point to it, though, since he almost exclusively uses his psychic perception already.

Question: On Darkrai and Cresselia.
Answer: I haven’t addressed Darkrai and Cresselia yet lol. There’s some hints in Chapter 54 though. Lugia does represent the moon’s influence on the planet. Along with some other things.

Question: You guys think Calanthia had the backing of a god when she started bringing Sinnoh back together?
Answer: She did not, but she did have the backing/was allies with the Aura Guardians of the time. And the Lake Orders.

Question: On how Lugia influenced Kyogre’s rest.
Answer: One thing to remember that hasn’t come up in a while is that Lugia’s basically erected seals around Kyogre’s resting place to prevent too much of its influence from leaking out and causing issues like Groudon’s is.

Question: On how Elf will adapt the movies.
Answer: I’m planning on incorporating elements from the movies - you can assume most of the basic stuff is the same - but actual execution will be extremely different haha. I think Spell of the Unown will be the last truly adapted movie.

Question: We already know japanese style names were the norm in today's Kanto and Johto, but did Sinnoh and Hoenn have similar styles as well?
Answer: Sort of! I’ve based Sinnoh a bit off of Greece, so you’d see more Greek-style names popping up back in the day, although there’s also some overlap introduced from relationships with Indigo and other regions - Indigo had a particular advantage in making contact with isolated places thanks to their abundant Pidgeot and the Wataru’s Dragonite. You see some elements of Indigo’s culture and naming conventions spreading due to that.

Question: Since they [Ranger Union] didn’t use pokeball, how did civilians tame Pokémon or help themselves when Rangers aren’t nearby?
Answer: The Ranger Union tends to have extremely close relationships with local Pokémon, partly a result of the cooperation between human and Pokémon during the Unovan incursions during the Last War. They’ve historically had great relationships, but that deepened it into something spectacular. Civilians oftentimes find partnerships with Pokémon without any pokeballs involved, although Rangers are definitely the experts in those situations.

Question: So Ranger Union territory is what N tried to achieve in Unova and the rest of the world?
Answer: Yep! Well, the Rangers sort of act like how Guardians of Indigo did in ancient times. It’s pretty common for them to have 1-2 extremely close companions, but recruiting other local Pokémon for additional support when needed.

Question: If Ash got his Teleport TM recharged could he teach it to Oz like he did infernus?
Answer: Hmm, if I remember correctly Teleport is a permanent TM (but it’s been a long time, so please correct me if I’m wrong). He can teach her at any time, although Oz doesn’t have quite as much psychic affinity so she couldn’t abuse it as heavily as Infernus. It would be a good addition to her repertoire, however.

Question: Were some of Kanto and Johto's clans formed from remnants of civilizations or broken clans migrating during that period, and settling in?
Answer: Yep! That’s a great observation. Northern territories like Rota, what was once Indigo City, Cerulean, Blackthorn, and Mahogany all received an influx of new blood, although admittedly there weren’t too many years for the migration south to take place, unfortunately.

Question: Necrozma is an ultra beast in Traveler right?
Answer: It’s actually a true Legend, unlike the other Ultra Beasts!

Question: On how far apart are all the Unown.
Answer: Earth is somewhat special, but on a cosmic scale it still only has the tiniest fraction of Unown. They’ve been scattered pretty much evenly all over the universe.

Question: How are Claydol made? Are they aura-crafted or something else?
Answer: So, basically that’s a situation where Mew’s boredom came into play. It showed the ancient people of the Arid Zone how to infuse Aura into their symbolic golems to breathe life into them - Baltoy and Claydol were basically like good fortune symbols or used like gargoyles initially until the first were animated, and were made to be able to self-replicate.

Question: On Mew’s appearance.
Answer: Mew is basically the softest thing in the universe. All the better to make friends!

Question: Has Mew changed its form often then? like if one culture sees pink cats as demonic omens would it change to blue cat when around them? [see question 949]
Answer: Yep! Mew basically just appeals to the audience. Which is helped by being a psychic god that can live in an entire nation’s head.

Question: Do actual paved roads between cities exist, and not just dirt paths?
Answer: Cars aren’t really a thing, though they do exist. Subways exist in cities, and trains were once extremely common, with a notable route going from Indigo, around the Dark World, up through the Ranger Union, and to Sinnoh. Railroads are extremely expensive to maintain since even a weak wild Pokémon could easily destroy or damage a section, so they rapidly fell out of popularity once matter-energy conversion became more common over the last century.

Question: Do quiet stealth helicopters exist like the military has?
Answer: I’d say most likely not, simply because there wouldn’t be a real need for them. Flying-types are already incredibly quick, agile, and quiet (when they want to be), and the real problem I think would be the relative strength of pokemon. One good Hyper Beam would probably be enough to seriously damage a helicopter like that, so they’d end up being too expensive for what they’re used for.

Question: Would you say that Noctowl are preferred for stealth?
Answer: Yep! Their silent flight, perception, and specialized psychic powers or things like Hypnosis are a huge bonus on that front.

Question: How hard is it to gather specific species to complete these tasks? Say Unova wanted a Noctowl squadron, would they formally request the Johtoan government for it or would they request prior trainers to donate eggs to train for their requirements?
Answer: No worries! Usually they’d rely on trainers who already have them and are associated with the Unovan League. Leagues tend to adapt to whatever their local Pokémon are, so you wouldn’t really see Leagues requesting specific pokemon. What you might see is trainers with Noctowl being loaned out to a friendly League temporarily.

Question: I was wondering if there existed trainers that lived out of RVs and if they could actually get around their region?
Answer: That would be a sight to see haha. But unfortunately RV’s aren’t really an option, although with storage technology you can basically make the equivalent of a cabin wherever you go.

Question: What's considered impressive for a rookie trainer to achieve in their first league? Like good and above average but not prodigy level?
Answer: Top 64. Maybe top 32. Making the top 16 in your first Conference is basically shorthand for being a future Master.

Question: Would you say that there are such thing as “late bloomers” for trainers? Those who start off slow but eventually reach the highest levels?
Answer: And absolutely! There are plenty of trainers who only start to take it seriously after a few years, or who slowly and steadily grow. Prodigy freaks like Ash, Gary, Amelia, or Jon are definitely the exception.Of course, you also have plenty of trainers who start out strong and jump ahead, only to either burn out or are unable to take their craft to the highest level. Lots of trainers who use powerful Pokémon but lack the ability to innovate or excel, so anyone truly skilled or creative can outfight them.

Question: So did Jon get scammed by his Fire Master?
Answer: Nah, I’d say he got exactly what he paid for. Jon got an extremely powerful technique, but since he doesn’t have the extremely deep knowledge of fire-types that a specialist or Master has he can’t take it to its fullest potential.

Question: If Delia ever became a trainer in her youth, what starter do you think she would have picked?
Answer: That’s a hard one! I don’t think she’d be in a position to get the standard Kanto starters, but I totally see her getting a relatively common mon and making it into something really special.

Question: On Blackthorn Gym’s purpose.
Answer: One thing to note is that Blackthorn is kind of designed to be brutal. It gatekeeps the Silver Conference much like Viridian gatekeeps the Indigo. Even the side gyms up there are significantly challenging. Manageable for a skilled trainer, but they don’t take it easy.

Question: How common is it for someone to not be a trainer at all?
Answer: I’d say around 99% of people have at least one Pokémon companion. Almost everyone has had some sort of training career, although not everyone goes on a journey and most never progress past the level of a trainer with 4-5 badges. Generally strong enough to protect themselves, but most wash out before they can become genuinely powerful.

Question: What do they call “ACE” trainers in Hoenn?
Answer: ACE Trainers are all called the same thing throughout the National League, but have different names in Kalos, Unova, and Galar. Unova has SEEK and Galar has NOBLE.

Question: On what constitutes a Pokemon trainer.
Answer: I use ‘trainer’ basically as a catchall for anyone who has a Pokémon and trains them somewhat, even if they don’t try to shoot for Conference. Pretty much everyone’s going to need at least one decently skilled Pokémon for many job opportunities, so there’s an incentive to train to at least the point your Pokémon can do something useful…plus most kids/early teens who are starting out think battling is the coolest thing ever lol.

Question: On whether the necessary infrastructure exists for Ash to post some dank memes.
Answer: Hmm so the internet isn’t very sophisticated/ not really a thing in Traveler at this point. It’s very, very primitive. Mostly downloadable databases - Porygon absolutely monitor League installations, but wouldn’t be involved in other unrelated ones (but corporations might have Porygon on loan to protect themselves).

Question: Is there a lot of focus on developing it [internet], either through League or private efforts? [see question 964]
Answer: Nope, not really a focus on developing it. Kind of like what [redacted] said - nobody’s quite even realized the potential at this point. They’re getting the most basic, practical use out of digital technologies at the moment, but I imagine over the next few years as regions become ever more connected that it might become more and more like our own.

Question: So how does the pokedex and email systems work in Traveler?
Answer: They do have connections between networks, but it’s pretty much only used for official League or commercial purposes.

Question: Who has the strongest trained Pokémon in Traveler and what is it?
Answer: Yep, I’d say Metagross is overall the strongest. The King amassed his power before Bruno caught him, so I don’t really count that.

Question: Who does Ash see as his biggest rival currently?
Answer: And yep, I’d say Will and Karen are his current benchmarks. They’re just out of reach after his time in Indigo.

Question: Do trainer have to go through gym rosters again every time a league came up? Or the badges they get from their old trip is good enough?
Answer: They have to go through gym rosters each time!

Question: On the Elite Four challenge.
Answer: My current system is basically that the Elite Four challenge takes place over at least 3-4 weeks. It would require that long for injuries to heal and for all Pokémon to be at their full power.

Question: We know that Blaine is a former member of the Elite Four, and we know the names of a few of the previous Champions of the Indigo League... but are there any other retired Elite Four members of note still around in Kanto and Johto?
Answer: There are undoubtedly several in retirement, but none alive that I’ve really put much thought into to be honest! That might be a fun thing for me to think about.

Question: Was it Mew's job as Guardian of Life that had it be the one who dealt with Mewtwo or was it just because Mewtwo was its clone? What does being the Guardian of life entail really, stopping  giant plagues or something?
Answer: Mew’s job as the Guardian of Life is strictly to protect life against things it can’t reasonably be expected to survive on its own - in practice, that means Legendaries. Plagues are fine, extinction of specific species is fine, but when a Legendary threatens life itself, it’s Mew’s responsibility to make sure life continues.

Question: On how Ash was able to resist Jirachi.
Answer: If I remember correctly, Ash didn’t need the Concepts to avoid falling victim to Jirachi.

Question: I know Traveler Orre doesn’t have any Wild Pokémon like in the games, but does it have relatively few or a regular amount for a region?
Answer: It has a smaller amount, but that number is gradually increasing!

Question: On how Orre compares to Alola.
Answer: Orre actually is the jewel of the Southern Continent, which is a tragedy.
